summary: Redesign Kamikakushi §1 around the estate-restoration vision with THREE factions (estate / villages / origin family-friends), tiers-to-Edo, non-invalid mediocre start, dream-as-origin-thread. Parallel facets → synthesize new §1 → adversarial verify.

canonical_s1.vision_statement: You are a mediocre young man — weak, slow, unskilled, and nameless — pulled half-drowned from a mountain river and taken in, out of plain kindness, by the Kurosawa: a proud, threadbare lower-samurai (goshi) house two generations past its grandeur and quietly drowning in debt. You begin as "another mouth," soft and clumsy, with no place at the table. Through nothing but daily toil and a refusal to quit, you earn your keep, then their trust, then your name on the household roster — and you climb, rung by sweat-bought rung, from taken-in stray to the lord's right hand and the architect of the house's revival. As you rise, the world you can reach grows with you: first the estate, then the valley village, then the region, then the domain's castle-town, and finally indirect recognition at the Edo capital itself, until the Kurosawa name stands restored AND surpassed beyond anything it held three generations ago. The estate-rise IS the perseverance fantasy at grand scale, and it is the same motion as the game's signature feature: the UI itself unlocks as you progress — minute one is a single line of narration, one verb, and a persistent event log, and every panel, tab, resource, skill, and area thereafter fades in one at a time, each reveal a story beat, never silent menu growth. Two grounded side-threads run quietly beneath the climb and pay off only in feeling, allies, and flavour — never in power: a grieving village is certain you are "Tama," their child spirited away by the kami ten years ago (the estate flatly disbelieves it; to them you are simply a farmhand), and a recurring dream slowly returns the memory of who you truly were and the living family and friends who will come to back you. Nothing supernatural ever happens; every omen resolves to a human cause; every climbing number is earned sweat. This is the dignity of a nobody — and a dying house — becoming someone real, by hand, and forcing a forgotten name to be spoken at the capital.
pillars: 1. THE ESTATE-RISE IS THE SPINE — a perseverance fantasy at grand scale. The single load-bearing track is the protagonist's climb up the Kurosawa service hierarchy (stray -> day-labourer -> bonded hand -> trusted hand -> foreman -> bailiff -> steward's deputy -> chief steward, the lord's right hand), fused to the house's outward expansion across five influence tiers (estate -> village -> region -> domain/castle-town -> Edo). Rising through the ranks and restoring-then-surpassing the house ARE the main quest; everything else enriches but never replaces it.

2. UI-AS-PROGRESSION — the interface is the reward. Start with one verb and a story log; reveal each panel/tab/resource/skill/area exactly when first earned, and announce every reveal diegetically through the event log, so feature unlocks read as plot, never menu inflation. The protagonist's accessible SPACE and accessible SYSTEMS literally ARE the progress bar of the rank climb; each tier opens a fresh, larger canvas with bigger numbers.

3. THREE FACTIONS, THREE SHAPES, ONE WEB. The estate (main, a discrete gated rank LADDER), the village (rich side faction, a continuous multi-node reputation WEB with the shoya, shops, craftsmen, artisans), and the origin (his living family/friends in a post-town, a memory-gated SUPPORT track) are deliberately distinct in shape and payoff so they never feel like one bar painted three colours — yet they are coupled by a shared economy and the Tama-vs-farmhand allegiance, and the side factions feed the spine without ever gating it.

4. NUMBERS-GO-UP IS A TRAINING MONTAGE YOU EARN — no hidden edge, ever. The protagonist starts genuinely weak and slow (not a bedridden invalid, but unskilled, low-stat, blistered-handed) and rises ONLY through repetition, rest, technique, and crafted tools. No bloodline, no secret training, no body that 'remembers' combat. Returning memory and the porter's-knot habit are identity clues that confer ZERO mechanical bonus. Combat capacity is gated behind labour-built conditioning; combat is the rare, dangerous exception, never a source of standing.

5. INFLUENCE IS THE MACRO-RESOURCE; TIERS REPLACE PRESTIGE — no reset of any kind. House Influence (家威, ka-i) is the estate's recognized standing, the single number the whole UI-reveal is gated on, accruing only when concrete achievements are recognized by the relevant authority. The long game is a pure tier escalation (each tier a bigger canvas and an order-of-magnitude bigger number range) — there is NO diegetic reset, NO prestige reset; everything persists as the canvas grows.

6. GROUNDED EDO FOLK-MYSTERY — folklore is light flavour, not the spine; quests are open-ended, not checklists. Every belief/omen resolves one-to-one to a human/natural cause; a thin, capped, off-screen margin of ambiguity is kept for unease only, never confirmed magic, never player magic. Folklore arrives as optional, light-hearted tidbits via the village inn's rumours board. Quests are a suggestion + a story the player goes and FINDS OUT in the world, never an A->B->C waypoint list; the dominant minute-to-minute behaviour is the incremental grind — the hero getting better at what he does.
canonical_s1.premise_and_tone: PREMISE: A mediocre young man wakes injured and nameless in the storehouse of the Kurosawa, a declining lower-samurai (goshi) house in a remote mountain valley, having been found half-drowned and pulled from the river out of plain kindness. The grieving village below is certain he is Tama, the child 'spirited away by the kami' ten years ago; the estate flatly disbelieves it — to the lord and his steward he is just a stray farmhand taken in out of decency. Through nothing but daily toil and a refusal to quit, he rises through the estate's service ranks from 'another mouth' to the lord's right hand and the architect of its revival, hauling the house from near-foreclosure up an outward climb of influence — valley, region, castle-town, and finally recognition at Edo — until the Kurosawa name is restored and surpassed. As he climbs, a recurring dream returns the memory of who he truly was, leading him back to the living family and friends of his old life, who come to support his achievements. Nothing supernatural ever happens; every omen has a human cause. TONE: grounded, warm, bittersweet Edo folk-mystery wrapped around a cozy, satisfying restoration fantasy. The dominant texture is peaceful Edo labour (farm, forage, woodcut, fish, craft) over a koku/rice base economy — numbers-go-up as honest sweat — with the steady dignity of a man and a house both rising from nothing. Beneath the warmth runs a quiet, grim undercurrent: a house bankrupted by an ancestor's hubris and cover-up, a village grieving a child it nearly sold, official negligence dressed as fate. The darkest material (a child nearly sold for debt, a drowned hamlet, a covered-up disaster) is handled with off-screen restraint and counter-weighted by found-family warmth (the Kurosawa household, Sayo, old Jubei). The catharsis is NEVER power — it is a nobody becoming someone real by his own hands, and lifting a dying house back into the light. Justice, where the side-mysteries reach it, is partial: the reachable culprits answer, the truly powerful largely escape — and the house's own rise meets a real, honest ceiling the powerful set, which is the same thesis made structural. Built as a pure, deterministic TypeScript core (one seeded RNG, data as single source of truth) with a thin DOM renderer, shipped as a static HTML5 build for itch.io.
canonical_s1.protagonist_and_mediocre_start:
THE PROTAGONIST is an AUTHORED, FIXED MALE character — there is NO name/appearance/gender creator and no player rename. He is the same person in every playthrough. The village calls him 'Tama' (the borrowed legend-name) for most of the game; his true name — Tahei, a plain, period-typical commoner name — returns to him as a late memory beat in the origin thread (no longer the spine's climax, since identity is explicitly a side thread; the reveal is authored and earned but de-emphasised relative to the estate climb). The fixed male gender is load-bearing: the village's legend has drifted to 'remember' the real lost child as a boy, when Tama was in fact a girl — a fair, re-readable clue that only works if the protagonist is fixed-male.

THE REFINED MEDIOCRE-START CONTRACT (locked, enforced in writing AND code): He is NOT a bedridden invalid at the open. He can rest and recover a little in the first hours, but he STARTS WEAK AND SLOW — unskilled, low-stat, soft-handed, slower than the boy who shows him the paddy, blistering on the first hoe. His one genuine asset is temperament: stubbornness, a strong back, a patient temper, a refusal to stop getting up — exactly what the incremental grind rewards. He grows ONLY through perseverance, repetition, rest, technique, and crafted tools. Plateaus are real; the answer to a wall is never a magic item, only more reps and better tools. Combat capacity is gated entirely behind labour-built conditioning ('the spear is just a long hoe held with intent') and the first real fight is humbling and near-fatal, motivating training.

NO-HIDDEN-EDGE GUARDRAILS (binding): (1) No hidden edge, ever — no bloodline, no secret training, no weapon that 'answers to him,' no body that 'remembers' combat. The one thing the village treats as special about him — that he is the returned Tama — is FALSE. (2) The porter's-knot rule — he sometimes ties a porter's/labourer's load-knot on instinct; this is procedural body-memory (like still knowing how to walk after amnesia), framed as a labourer's habit ('huh, you've hauled before'), a NARRATIVE IDENTITY CLUE ONLY that confers ZERO mechanical bonus. (3) Growth is grind, not gift — every number starts at the bottom; no returning memory or instinctive habit ever grants a starting stat, recipe, tool, or combat bonus. (4) The talent-foil antagonists (the enforcer Hanzaki, the rival heir, the rival house) must always be shown as TRAINED, lucky, or ruthless — never innately gifted in a way the protagonist could not match by work — so the effort-over-talent thesis is never undercut.

estate.summary: THE KUROSAWA GOSHI HOUSEHOLD is the MAIN faction and the spine. The Kurosawa are a lower-samurai (goshi) house holding a modest hill estate in Asagiri valley — proud, threadbare, two generations past their grandeur, working much of their own land, and quietly drowning in a ruinous debt inherited from a grandfather's failed flood-control venture. This is the ONLY faction structured as a discrete, named, gated RANK LADDER with hard thresholds, because rising through it IS the perseverance fantasy and it is the dominant driver of the UI-as-progression reveal. Standing is earned by Estate Service (a faction-XP pool from completed duties and survived seasons) AND by authored 'trust beats' (named story gates), so you can never grind a number to skip the narrative — rank is where numbers-go-up and story fuse. Each promotion simultaneously fires through the universal rewards/unlock bus: a diegetic log line, the next panel/area reveal, a concrete perk, and a story flag. The household is genuinely POPULATED (a full roster), and it flatly DISBELIEVES the kamikakushi legend — to them he is a reliable hand, not a returned spirit. The estate generates House Influence directly; the capstone of the local ladder is the diegetic BRIDGE that opens each next tier (the protagonist, as the lord's right hand, now projects the house's influence outward).

rank_ladder.how_earned: Found half-drowned and taken in; survive convalescence and the first labour. (Already met at the open.)
rank_ladder.unlocks: The kura storehouse (one room, one verb); the body/rest bar and rice counter. The estate dashboard begins bare and diegetic.

rank_ladder.how_earned: Chief Steward Genemon, who calls him 'another mouth, soft and clumsy,' assigns the first real work; complete it and earn a sleeping-place.
rank_ladder.unlocks: The gate & forecourt; the home paddies and dry fields (the rice/koku heartbeat); the basic labour loop and first producers.

rank_ladder.how_earned: Sustained, reliable labour across a season; Genemon grants a place on the household's books.
rank_ladder.unlocks: Foraging, woodcutting, hauling; the stables & woodlot; the porter's-knot beat surfaces. Skills tab reveals.

rank_ladder.how_earned: Win Lady Chiyo's regard for indoor work and the heir Naoyuki's grudging vouching; complete authored trust beats (e.g. defend the grain store, return a lost ledger).
rank_ladder.unlocks: The main house interior; the household domestic economy (textiles, kitchen, provisioning); errands beyond the estate (the village unlocks here). The drill yard opens after the humbling first fight; training/idle-combat under Jubei.

rank_ladder.how_earned: Demonstrate command of a labour-gang and a managed sub-economy; the first shinden reclamation begun.
rank_ladder.unlocks: The workshops and granary; a labour-gang to assign; the first managed sub-economy; +1 assignable helper slot. The assignment/management panel reveals.

rank_ladder.how_earned: The first reclamation recorded and the house edging toward solvency; the lord begins to notice.
rank_ladder.unlocks: The granary and the estate's field administration; the House Influence macro-resource is now visible and tracked; cash-crop and proto-industry levers come online.

rank_ladder.how_earned: Help clerk Tanomo balance the books and restructure the strangling debt; Genemon comes to rely on him; the lord Munenori's explicit recognition.
rank_ladder.unlocks: The lord's study and the ledgers; the estate's whole books; the tier-expansion map; +X% Influence per season. The village tier becomes a reachable canvas.

rank_ladder.rank: 7 — Chief steward / the lord's right hand (karo-kaku)
rank_ladder.how_earned: The top a non-born man can plausibly reach by merit — sustained service, the house's solvency secured, and a merit-elevation or symbolic adoption into the house name (the capstone). Genemon names him deputy and eventual successor.
rank_ladder.unlocks: The estate gate to the wider world; authority to project the house's influence outward. This capstone is the diegetic BRIDGE that opens the higher tiers (region/domain/Edo); finishing the ladder resets NOTHING — it opens the next, larger canvas.

villages.summary: THE VILLAGE OF ASAGIRI is a rich SIDE faction with its own problems the player may choose to help — a feared ford, a skimming foreman, debt owed to the estate, and the unhealed grief over the lost child. It is deliberately shaped UNLIKE the estate so the two feel different in the hand: instead of one rank ladder it is a continuous, MULTI-NODE reputation web. It is the home of the kamikakushi / 'are you Tama?' legend, which the villagers BELIEVE and the estate does not. Folklore lives here as LIGHT flavour, delivered through the inn's rumours board. The village's deepest optional side-quest is uncovering what really happened to the real lost child. CRUCIALLY, village standing NEVER gates the UI ladder or the tier climb — a player who ignores the village is never blocked on the spine, only poorer and lonelier; the village is a viable-but-poorer playstyle to skip, never a wall.
villages.reputation_design: Several continuous meters rather than one ladder: (1) PER-SHOP 'patron/regular' standing (the smith, the dry-goods/rice broker, the herbalist's stall, the brewer, the weaver) — each unlocks stock, discounts, and exclusives as you trade, and high standing SOFTENS the market-saturation price-crash from flooding a good. (2) PER-FAMILY goodwill, raised by open-ended help (you hear a family has trouble and go figure out how to help — never a checklist). (3) An ARTISANS'/CRAFT-GUILD standing gating recipes, component tiers, and master-craft commissions. (4) The VILLAGE CHIEF's regard — historically the SHOYA/NANUSHI headman, Yagoemon, drawn from the local elite and assisted by the village's self-governing body (kumigashira, hyakushodai) under murauke — a weighted roll-up of the others plus chief-specific quests. Village reputation unlocks RECIPES, CRAFTING TIERS, TRADE (prices, exclusives, contracts), ALLIES (villagers who can be seconded to the estate as idle producers/co-producers), and LORE (the rumours-board folklore and clues for the lost-child thread). Curves are GENTLE (linear/soft-cap) for frequent small dopamine across many nodes, in contrast to the estate's steep geometric rank gates. Yagoemon does honest civic double-duty: warm quest-giver up front, and the humanly-reachable culprit of the lost-child side-mystery later (a frightened middle-man who doctored ledgers and let the 'cursed' story stand — never a monster).

origin.summary: THE ORIGIN is the protagonist's ORIGINAL FAMILY & FRIENDS — LIVING people in a nearby post-town (Magome-juku, a day-plus down the kaido road), NOT the local village. His true name is Tahei; his old life was as a young porter/errand-hand for a small transport-and-goods house. This is a SIDE faction unlocked as his memory returns via the recurring dream, and its defining feature is that it SUPPORTS him for his effort and achievements at the estate — pride, allies, resources, and trade ties that plug into the estate's outward expansion. It is the third faction made concrete: reconnecting with his old life converts emotional payoff into hard macro assets that FEED the spine rather than competing with it. This broadens the older 'family dead, drowned hamlet' framing: per the locked 'they SUPPORT him' direction, the family and friends are alive; the upstream hamlet of Kuzuhara, his birthplace, becomes a re-foundable region-tier node rather than a dead end.
origin.design: Structured DIFFERENTLY from the estate and village: a memory-gated, late-blooming SUPPORT track, NOT grindable from day one. It stays dark (foreshadowed for hours, so its absence reads as anticipation) until the recurring dream returns enough memory AND the protagonist's estate standing lets him physically travel the controlled kaido (sekisho checkpoints make free travel impossible without standing). It then opens as a set of per-contact 'restored ties' — discrete, chunky one-time milestones (mother Oyuki, sister Okimi, employer Denbei, friend Kanta, sweetheart Ohana, the porter-guild), re-engaged in AUTHORED beats rather than a from-zero grind. Payoff is deliberately SUPPORT, not local power: (a) PRIDE/MORALE — a modest GLOBAL skill-XP/producer buff framed strictly as a NEW present-day relationship ('a man with people behind him works harder'), NEVER a retroactive gift from remembering; (b) ALLIES — old porter/guild mates who can be recruited to the expansion; (c) TRADE TIES — origin-town goods, contracts, and ready-made caravan/porter routes that plug into the estate's region/domain/Edo expansion. As the protagonist reaches the region tier, the reconnected contacts and the re-foundable Kuzuhara become concrete expansion NODES (a resettlement labour/producer base; the post-town as trade partner and information broker; the embankment that killed his caravan as a Tier-3 river-works Influence project that also resolves his backstory). HARD GUARDRAIL (D-003): returning memory grants ACCESS only (new nodes/allies/quests unlock narratively) and ZERO mechanical bonus; every asset must still be grind-built like everything else. At least one origin beat is always available WITHOUT reputation-gating so the thread never fully stalls behind the grind. The track is allegiance-NEUTRAL — it is about who he REALLY is (neither Tama nor merely-a-farmhand) — and resolving it softens both the Tama and farmhand leans by giving him a true self to stand on. Curves use DISCRETE milestone steps (each restored tie a chunky one-time unlock), distinct from the estate's geometric gates and the village's gentle per-node meters.

factions.interrelation: The three tracks share an economy and the Tama-vs-farmhand allegiance, with chosen tensions AND synergies, and no faction is ever a dead end. SEPARATE CURRENCIES keep them from collapsing into one bar: Estate Service XP (steep geometric, per-duty caps, paces big rank gates), Village Reputation (gentle per-node meters), and Origin Ties (discrete memory milestones). Above all three sits HOUSE INFLUENCE, the macro-resource the estate spends to expand: the estate generates it directly, while village allies and origin trade-ties act as MULTIPLIERS/FEEDERS — they don't unlock the next tier, they make conquering it faster and cheaper (tuned so weaving both in roughly halves time-to-next-tier — felt, never a wall). TENSIONS (chosen, not bugs): (a) high estate rank can hand duties that EXTRACT from the village (collect the rice quota), raising Estate Service but denting Village reputation, with an always-available 'soften it quietly' option the side-mystery rewards; (b) the legend — the village wants 'Tama' to be theirs while the estate wants a reliable hand, so being beloved in one can make the other wary of split loyalty; (c) the origin pulls him down-valley while the estate spine pulls him deeper in. SYNERGIES: village allies become idle producers seconded to estate works; origin trade-ties become the supply chains that make region/Edo expansion affordable; high estate rank grants the standing and safe-passage to even reach the origin town and trade with it formally. THE TAMA-VS-FARMHAND ALLEGIANCE is a continuous LEANING (village-leaning <-> estate-leaning, default neutral, re-expressible and never frozen) the player nudges through dialogue and which faction he invests labour in. Leaning 'I am Tama' warms the village (faster rep, grief-coded content, Bon rites) at slight friction with the estate (the estate finds the legend a superstitious distraction — slightly slower trust beats, NEVER blocked). Leaning 'just a farmhand' smooths estate trust and pragmatic respect at the cost of village warmth and Tama-only lore. CRITICAL RULE: the choice rebalances RATES and FLAVOUR, never AVAILABILITY — both factions remain fully completable on either lean; neither is the 'good' path; staying ambivalent is a valid in-character stance. The endgame thesis: the spine is restored and surpassed FASTEST and richest when all three are woven together — but it is still completable spine-only, just slower and lonelier.

canonical_s1.influence_macro_resource: HOUSE INFLUENCE (家威, ka-i) is the single tracked macro-resource and the one number the entire UI-reveal is gated on. It is the estate's RECOGNIZED STANDING in the eyes of progressively wider authorities — deliberately NOT koku and NOT coin. Koku and coin are the inputs you spend and grind; Influence is the cumulative, mostly-monotonic score of what the estate has visibly BECOME. It accrues only when a concrete achievement is RECOGNIZED by the relevant authority for the current tier: reclaiming a paddy adds koku immediately, but only converts to Influence when the village/steward/domain acknowledges it (a recorded yield, a granted title, a sealed contract, a won petition). This keeps Influence diegetic ('the books now show...') and makes it the gate-currency — every panel/tab/system/area unlock in the incremental reveal carries an Influence (plus flag/skill) predicate, so the macro spine literally paces the UI. Influence has named BANDS mapping 1:1 to the five tiers; crossing a band is the tier-up moment. It NEVER resets (locked: no prestige). It can STALL at a tier gate you haven't met, and a small, scripted set of beats can DENT it (a debt called, a scandal) — small, recoverable, never a wipe — to keep tension. THE CONVERSION LEDGER turns grind into Influence via authentic Edo upward-mobility levers, so 'numbers go up' always reads as a real path a small declining goshi house could climb: (1) SHINDEN land reclamation (the canonical goshi-advancement lever — a granted increase in recognized yield is the purest Influence source); (2) cash crops & proto-industry (sericulture/silk, sake brewing, textile weaving as village by-employments the estate organizes and takes a cut of, building toward a famous domain product/meibutsu); (3) river & road works (fords, embankments, kaido upkeep — protect yield AND earn official credit as distinguished service); (4) debt restructuring (moving from a strangling usurious loan to proper merchant finance / a domain loan — liability into solvency into creditworthiness, until the estate extends credit instead of begging it); (5) distinguished SERVICE to the domain (relief-rice in a famine, flood control, a petition resolved in the house's favour); (6) patronage & alliance (strategic marriage and the very real Edo practice of merchant/farmer buy-in via goyokin contributions and ADOPTION into samurai rank, plus patrons who vouch upward); (7) trade ties (routes to the post-town, the origin town, and onward to the inter-han / Osaka market). Each lever is a small economic sub-loop (spend koku/coin/labour -> produce -> get recognized -> Influence), and which levers are AVAILABLE is itself part of the tier reveal. Mechanically Influence is one more resource in GameState (a monotonic-ish counter plus a band index), produced by the same recognized-achievement events the rewards/unlock bus already emits — no new subsystem, just a conversion ledger and a band table, all deterministic under the one seeded RNG, with thresholds/weights generated into docs/.

main_quest_tiers.name: ESTATE
main_quest_tiers.summary: Earn your keep and a place at the table. The starting micro-world: one declining hill estate in Asagiri valley, unlocked room by room as trust rises.
main_quest_tiers.gate: Survive convalescence and the first labour (already met at the open).
headline_beats: Be found half-drowned, taken in, and survive convalescence in the kura storehouse (one room, one verb).
Become a trusted hand under Chief Steward Genemon, climbing the early rungs of the rank ladder.
Discover the house is quietly insolvent — a merchant loan/goyokin coming due, fields fallow, the staff thinned.
Propose and begin the first shinden (new-paddy) reclamation; the dowager Toku and clerk Tanomo part with pieces of why the house fell.
Survive the humbling, near-fatal first fight; beg old Jubei for drills (training/idle-combat unlocks).

main_quest_tiers.name: VILLAGE
main_quest_tiers.summary: The estate as a presence in its own valley. The first expansion frontier: the nearby village of Asagiri — its shops, craftsmen, artisans, inn, shrine, and the kamikakushi legend.
main_quest_tiers.gate: First reclamation recorded + the house solvent enough to extend credit rather than beg it (and the estate trusts the protagonist to run errands beyond its gate).
headline_beats: The estate starts buying village output and contracting craftsmen; the protagonist builds reputation with Headman Yagoemon and the village's self-governing body.
Cash crops and proto-industry come online (sericulture, sake, textiles) with the estate organizing and taking a cut.
A river/ford works project protects yield and earns official credit.
The estate becomes the valley's economic anchor; its mon appears on village contracts; the first proper retainers are taken on.
The optional lost-child side-mystery and the inn's rumours board (light folklore) open here; the Tama-vs-farmhand allegiance is most legible.

main_quest_tiers.name: REGION
main_quest_tiers.summary: Reach beyond the home valley. A fresh, larger canvas: a cluster of valleys, the down-valley post-town on the kaido, the upstream Kuzuhara ruins, roads, porters, and sekisho checkpoints.
main_quest_tiers.gate: Village anchored + a tradeable surplus + roads/porters under estate control (and enough standing to travel the controlled roads).
headline_beats: Open trade routes along the kaido; the dream-gated origin faction (faction 3) becomes reachable and its trade ties plug in.
RE-FOUND KUZUHARA as a resettlement — the protagonist's origin hamlet becomes a region expansion node, its returning people a labour/trade base (the faction-3 fusion, access-only, grind-built).
Secure the embankment so the disaster cannot recur — a river-works Influence project that also resolves the protagonist's backstory.
The post-town becomes a trade partner and information broker; pack-trains and a branch holding appear; the estate's name travels.

main_quest_tiers.name: DOMAIN / CASTLE-TOWN
main_quest_tiers.summary: Be reckoned with by the people who actually rule. The canvas: the castle-town, the domain's officials (daikan, tedai), inter-han markets, Osaka rice shipping.
main_quest_tiers.gate: Distinguished SERVICE rendered to the domain — flood-control, surplus rice in a lean year, or a formal case/petition resolving in the house's favour.
headline_beats: Invited (warily) into domain affairs through the daikan->tedai->shoya administrative chain.
Restructure the old debt through proper merchant finance / a daimyo loan rather than usury; the house becomes creditworthy.
A strategic marriage or adoption alliance, and goyokin contributions, lift the house's formal standing (the most authentic top-rung lever).
The estate's goods reach Osaka and become a renowned domain product; the steward is installed at the castle-town; the house name appears on inter-han ledgers.

main_quest_tiers.name: EDO
main_quest_tiers.summary: Recognition at the capital — restore AND surpass the grandeur of three generations ago. The canvas: Edo, the bakufu status ladder, the realm's markets.
main_quest_tiers.gate: Domain sponsorship + enough standing to be presented (carried via the lord's sankin-kotai attendance).
headline_beats: The house's renowned domain product reaches and is celebrated in Edo's markets, carried by the sankin-kotai supply chains.
The protagonist's labour materially lifts the LORD'S standing during his Edo attendance, so the HOUSE gains face at the capital.
A rare, hard-won merit commendation or appointment is earned by distinguished, documented service (the authentic exceptional ceiling).

The restored-and-surpassed house seal; an epilogue tableau of everything built. The CEILING is honoured: 'Edo recognition' is indirect and mediated (a famous product, the lord's lifted standing, a rare appointment), NOT the protagonist personally becoming a hatamoto or being received by the shogun — the reachable is reached, the truly powerful remain above, which is the partial-justice thesis made structural.

canonical_s1.identity_and_origin_threads:
Two grounded side-threads run quietly beneath the estate-rise spine; both pay off only in feeling, allies, and flavour, never power, and both resolve to human causes with no magic.

(A) THE TAMA-VS-FARMHAND ALLEGIANCE SIDE-THREAD. The valley grieves a child, Tama, 'spirited away by the kami' ten years ago; a half-drowned, memoryless young man washing up below the estate reads to them as the kami giving Tama back. The estate flatly disbelieves it — to the lord and steward he is a stray farmhand taken in out of decency. This split powers a continuous ALLEGIANCE LEANING (not a one-time branch, not a character-creator): the player nudges village-leaning <-> estate-leaning through ordinary dialogue and which faction he invests labour in. It only ever shifts standing-gain RATES and FLAVOUR and which dialogue/quest-givers open first — NEVER stats, drops, or production, and never availability (both factions stay fully completable on either lean). He may stay ambivalent the whole game (a valid in-character stance: he genuinely doesn't know). Leaning-Tama pays off in the village's embrace, grief-coded content, and a heavier reckoning when the truth lands; leaning-farmhand pays off in the estate's respect for an honest, self-made hand. This is the mechanical home of the locked 'who you are is a SIDE thread, roleplay not creator' decision.

(B) THE GROUNDED TRUE-ORIGIN THREAD (faction 3). His real past is mundane and, per the locked 'they SUPPORT him' direction, NOT a dead end: his true name is Tahei, and he was a young porter/errand-hand for a small transport-and-goods house in Magome-juku, a post-town a day-plus down the kaido (NOT the local village). His LIVING family and friends are there — broadened from the older 'family dead' framing to surviving kin (mother Oyuki, sister Okimi), his old employer (Master Denbei), childhood friends (Kanta), a sweetheart (Ohana), and his porter-guild mates — so there are real people to reconnect with and to back the estate. As memory returns he re-engages them; seeing what he is rebuilding at the estate, they support him with pride, allies, resources, and trade ties that feed the outward expansion. Recovering who he was literally expands the house — but via ACCESS only (new nodes/allies/quests), never a mechanical gift.

THE GROUNDED LOST-CHILD TRUTH (the village's deepest optional side-segment, open-ended, not a checklist). 'What happened to Tama?' is a suggestion + a story the player pulls on and discovers in the world — clues stumbled upon through labour, travel, and rising village trust (a child's straw sandal left yearly at the shrine; a household that flinches at her name; the legend 'remembering' a boy when an old woman misremembers 'she'; a midwife's reluctance; a peddler's road-gossip; a debt-ledger mention; a post-town sighting). The truth: Tama was a GIRL (the gender-drift is the fair clue) who RAN from a violent stepfather and a household about to sell her into service to clear a rice debt owed to the Kurosawa estate; a midwife and the peddler Sokichi helped her flee; she has been ALIVE and grown the whole decade in the down-valley post-town (under the name Oharu), too ashamed to return. The adults let 'the kami took her' stand because a comforting miracle was easier than 'we were about to sell a child.' Resolution is grounded and partial — reuniting the valley with the living, grown Tama is costly and incomplete (she may not forgive the home that nearly sold her). Notably, the SAME rice debt-and-quota machine that nearly sold Tama is the root that menaces the village and that wrote off Tahei's caravan, and it traces back to the Kurosawa grandfather's covered-up disaster — so the side-mysteries, the origin, and the house's backstory share one rotten root.

THE GROUNDED ORIGIN STORY (how he reached the estate, no magic). Tahei left Magome-juku on a mundane errand — escorting a goods consignment over the mountain pass for his employer Denbei. The journey went wrong: a flash flood (a neglected upstream embankment failing) and rockfall on the fog-blind pass struck the small caravan; he was struck, half-drowned, swept downriver, and snagged at a weir below the Kurosawa estate. His amnesia is ordinary head trauma + near-drowning + exposure — no magic. The estate physician Ranpo dresses his healing scalp wound, names symptoms not visions, and flatly disbelieves the kami story, grounding the mundane cause. (The guildmaster Zenroku wrote him off as dead to hide that the caravan ran short-weighted/contraband cargo, tying the origin into the same racket.)
canonical_s1.the_dream: THE RECURRING DREAM is the thread that leads back to faction 3 (his living family and friends), and it is reframed strictly as RETURNING AUTOBIOGRAPHICAL MEMORY — never clairvoyance, never a psychic tether, and conferring ZERO mechanical bonus. Rules, enforced in writing AND code: (1) MEMORY-ONLY — the dream returns only things he actually lived (his name Tahei, a face, a road, the smell of paddy-mud, the porter's-knot habit's origin, Magome-juku, a person's voice in the water). It NEVER surfaces external facts he could not have known firsthand — no precognition, no clairvoyance. The 'voice in the water' is a real person he heard and is now recalling, not a spirit. (2) ZERO MECHANICAL BONUS — a returning memory or instinctive habit never grants a starting stat, recipe, tool, or combat edge (binding the porter's-knot rule). What memory grants is ACCESS: the origin faction's nodes, allies, and quests unlock narratively, and the assets behind them must still be grind-built like everything else. (3) GUARANTEED CADENCE — at least one origin/dream fragment is delivered within a capped number of in-game days OR a skill/standing threshold, and at least one origin-thread beat is always available WITHOUT reputation-gating, so the thread never goes cold or fully stalls behind the grind. Each fragment is a short authored sensory beat in the event log; the renderer just shows the deterministic-core flag firing (a per-day scheduler checks days-since-last-fragment against a cap and fires an authored line + story flag; all variance flows through the single seeded RNG). The cadence cap and the always-available ungated beat are tunable content constraints, headlessly regression-testable via the DEV play API (force a late standing, fast-forward days, assert a fragment fired within N days AND that no stat/recipe was granted).
canonical_s1.folklore_handling: Folklore is LIGHT FLAVOUR, NOT the spine — the estate-rise is the spine. The campaign is NOT built around named yokai; folklore arrives occasionally as small, light-hearted optional tidbits, concentrated in one diegetic hub: the village INN'S RUMOURS BOARD. Each rumour is a lightweight yokai story the player MAY go investigate, open-endedly, out in the world (kappa at the ford, tanuki stealing rice, fox-fire on the ridge, a one-eyed mountain god of the pass, yamanba of the high woods, tengu of the high cedars). HARD RULE: every rumour-quest is OPTIONAL and NONE gate main-quest tier progression. Every folk belief is introduced as GENUINE, respected village dread first, investigated through ordinary work, and resolved one-to-one to a concrete human/natural cause via a belief->cause table — but the game LINGERS in the unease before resolving, and debunks with DAWNING DREAD, never a Scooby-Doo unmasking. The master belief, kamikakushi, is itself a comforting lie covering a human truth (people who fled debt/violence/conscription, or died on a lethal fog-blind road — and the protagonist's own flood trauma); handled as grief-coping, not superstition to mock. RESIDUAL AMBIGUITY is hard-capped at ~1-2 unresolved, off-screen, mundane-readable beats (recommend the unidentified-hand offering at the boundary-stone where he was found as the one left open); the 'helpful fox' is de-fanged to flatly explicable behaviour. Nothing is ever confirmed supernatural; nothing is mechanized; there is NEVER player magic. Folk-religion texture is specific and respectful, distinguishing Shinto (shrine, shimenawa, boundary/mountain kami, the soul-calling rite) from Buddhist elements (roadside/boundary jizo as guardians of children and travelers — fitting both the lost child and the protagonist found at a boundary-stone — Bon offerings, the temple register of the vanished that becomes hard evidence) rather than blurring into generic 'Shinto magic.' No rite ever mechanically 'works.'

cast.name: Lord Kurosawa Munenori
cast.role: Head of the Kurosawa house, late 50s; weary, decent, stiff-backed pride papering over shame at the decline his father's father caused.
cast.function: Apex rank-gatekeeper — promotions to the upper rungs require his explicit recognition, earned by sustained service and quietly hauling the house out of debt. His approval IS the main quest's measure; his eventual trust is the emotional payoff. Believes ledgers, not omens; to him the protagonist is 'a farmhand we took in.'

cast.name: Kurosawa Naoyuki
cast.role: The lord's son and heir, ~22; talented, restless, chafing at genteel poverty.
cast.function: Early-game RIVAL inside the household — gatekeeps the mid rungs (must vouch before his father will) and at first withholds. The talent-foil INSIDE the family (things came easily, so he disdains plodding effort) until the protagonist's grind outpaces his coasting. Arc: rivalry -> grudging respect -> brotherhood; by the upper tiers they drive the expansion together. Converted-talent, not innate gift.

cast.name: Lady Kurosawa Chiyo
cast.role: The lord's wife, ~50; manages the inner household and its meagre purse, gracious but exacting.
cast.function: Gatekeeps houseman access (rung 3) and the household's domestic economy (textiles, kitchen, hospitality). Winning her regard unlocks indoor work and the estate's influence-entertaining systems used at higher tiers.

cast.name: Dowager Kurosawa Toku
cast.role: The lord's mother, ~75; sharp-memoried, the only one who lived through the house's fall as an adult.
cast.function: Living BACKSTORY KEEPER — holds and slowly parts with the truth of why the house declined (the grandfather Sadamune's flood-venture and the lost favour). Relationship/lore quest-giver; embodies 'no shortcuts' (rebuilt daily survival by hand after the fall); wry about the village's kami-fuss.

cast.name: Chief Steward Genemon
cast.role: The karo-equivalent chief steward who actually runs the estate day to day, ~60; dry, overworked, fiercely loyal, exhausted by holding a sinking ledger together.
cast.function: The SPINE'S PRIMARY rank-gatekeeper and quest-giver — first calls the protagonist 'another mouth, soft and clumsy,' assigns nearly all early labour, and grants the rung-by-rung promotions. Most-interacted estate NPC. Arc: grudging tolerance -> reliance -> naming the protagonist his trusted deputy and successor (the literal rise to the top). The estate's old rice-debt records pass through him, opening the documentary thread later.

cast.name: Tanomo
cast.role: The estate accountant/clerk, ~45; keeps the books.
cast.function: Gatekeeps the koku/economy and debt-repayment systems; the in-house thread into the ledger/debt mystery — helping him balance the books materially saves the estate and surfaces old entries.

cast.name: Jubei
cast.role: Aging estate master-at-arms / drillmaster, competent-but-never-great old foot-soldier with bad knees.
cast.function: THE MENTOR and combat/training gatekeeper; speaks the creed 'Talent is a story the lucky tell. You are not lucky. So you will work.' — chief embodiment of effort-over-talent. Gates the entire training/idle-combat suite after the humbling first fight. Carries famine-era guilt; his decline seeds the late-tier 'you now teach others' layer.

cast.name: Saburozaemon
cast.role: Stern senior retainer, ~55; traditional, exacting.
cast.function: Gatekeeps respect at the retainer rungs; withholds approval, warms last and means it most.

cast.name: Heisuke
cast.role: Friendly peer retainer, ~30; open-handed.
cast.function: Shows the ropes of retainer life and becomes a true friend; low-friction quest-giver, morale anchor, the 'we're in this together' counterweight.

cast.name: Kanbei
cast.role: Jealous middling retainer, ~35; cornered and proud, not evil.
cast.function: In-household ANTAGONIST-RIVAL who sees the rising stray as a threat to his own scarce advancement; sabotages and spreads doubt. Arc: grudging detente or he washes out by his own bitterness — player-influenceable. Differentiated from Naoyuki: Naoyuki is the family insider you win over; Kanbei is the peer who may never come around.

cast.name: Tokujiro
cast.role: Green recruit, ~16; joins after the protagonist has risen and looks up to him.
cast.function: The mirror that proves the protagonist's growth; seeds the late-tier 'you now teach others from zero' layer (re-homing the old reset's teaching idea), the way Jubei once started the protagonist.

cast.name: Oai
cast.role: Head maidservant, ~40; runs the indoor staff and the valley's servant-gossip network.
cast.function: Quest-giver and information broker INSIDE the house.

cast.name: Kyusuke
cast.role: Estate cook, ~50; warm comic relief.
cast.function: Runs the food/provisioning sub-economy; a soft daily-life anchor.

cast.name: Sota & Mago
cast.role: A grizzled groom and a cheeky teen field-labourer — the bottom-rung peers the protagonist starts beside.
cast.function: The field/stable labour loop and honest friendship at the floor of the ladder.

cast.name: Ranpo
cast.role: Estate physician, ~55; rational, plain-spoken.
cast.function: Dresses the protagonist's healing scalp wound (grounding the mundane amnesia), names symptoms not visions, gates healing/medicine systems; flatly disbelieves the kami story.

cast.name: Headman Yagoemon
cast.role: Village headman (shoya/nanushi), drawn from the local elite; juggles nengu quotas, corvee, disputes, and relief.
cast.function: The Village Chief reputation gatekeeper (grants village standing and village-tier expansion access) AND the humanly-reachable culprit of the lost-child side-mystery (a frightened middle-man who doctored ledgers and let the 'cursed' story stand). Warm civic quest-giver up front; reachable answer-for-it figure at the climax — never a monster.

cast.name: Sayo
cast.role: The headman's daughter, ~16; sharp, kind, keeper of the valley's news and rumours.
cast.function: The village's main quest-giver, emotional anchor, and the LIVING HEART OF THE RED HERRING — names the protagonist 'Tama' on sight and needs him to be her lost friend. Tutorializes the village/trade loop; channels rumour-as-gameplay; the person the truth most hurts and most frees.

cast.name: Obaa Sato
cast.role: Village herbalist / wise-woman.
cast.function: The village's folklore-keeper — narrates the kamikakushi legend sympathetically while knowing it is coping; teaches foraging/village-craft; the warm face of the village's belief.

cast.name: Priest Ryoa
cast.role: The shrine/temple keeper; runs the soul-calling rite and keeps a register of the vanished.
cast.function: Folklore-atmosphere quest-giver whose records become hard evidence in the lost-child thread; embodies grief-work that yields truth.

cast.name: Smith Gonta
cast.role: Gruff village blacksmith.
cast.function: Tools and later spearheads — gates metalcraft; values shown effort over flash.

cast.name: Carpenter Risuke
cast.role: Village carpenter/builder.
cast.function: Repairs and construction — gates building systems used to rebuild and expand the estate.

cast.name: Weaver Onatsu
cast.role: Village weaver, a sharp widow.
cast.function: Cloth/textile trade — ties into Lady Chiyo's household economy and origin-town trade routes.

cast.name: Brewer Tokuemon
cast.role: Village sake brewer, jovial.
cast.function: The village's festival/social hub; his brew greases reputation; a flavour-rich relationship.

cast.name: Innkeeper Sukezo
cast.role: Runs the village inn and the RUMOURS BOARD.
cast.function: The dedicated delivery vector for optional light-folklore side-quests (each rumour a lightweight yokai story to investigate, resolving grounded); information broker.

cast.name: Peddler Sokichi
cast.role: Itinerant medicine-seller / peddler.
cast.function: The engine that lets the player chase clues and trade BEYOND the village (the bridge toward the origin town and the regional tier); grounded source of yokai tales; helped the real Tama flee years ago (a lost-child clue).

cast.name: Foreman Magobei
cast.role: A skimming village foreman — the 'tanuki' of the rumours board.
cast.function: Notable ordinary-villager-turned-petty-antagonist; his theft is the first hard thread into the larger rice-quota racket. Reachable and human.

cast.name: Oyuki
cast.role: Tahei's widowed mother in Magome-juku, ~45.
cast.function: Emotional core of the origin thread — grieved him as dead; the reunion is the warm payoff of the memory arc, kept earned and a little costly (she is furious he was written off, and at first he barely remembers her).

cast.name: Okimi
cast.role: Tahei's elder sister, ~20; practical, married into a Magome trading family.
cast.function: The concrete TRADE-TIE that lets the origin town supply and route goods for the estate's expansion. (Renamed from the facet's 'Okiku' to avoid collision with the superseded Kuzuhara survivor 'Kiku'.)

cast.name: Master Denbei
cast.role: Tahei's old employer, ~55; runs the small transport-and-goods house.
cast.function: Proud of the diligent boy he trained; supplies porter/logistics know-how and legitimate manifests — quest-giver and the grounded source of the porter's-knot identity (he taught it; it confers ZERO bonus).

cast.name: Kanta
cast.role: Tahei's childhood best friend and fellow porter, ~18.
cast.function: Comic-warm friendship rekindled; an ally who can be recruited to help the expansion.

cast.name: Ohana
cast.role: A sweetheart Tahei half-remembers, ~17.
cast.function: Optional relationship thread the dream surfaces; grounded and gentle, no melodrama.

cast.name: Guildmaster Zenroku
cast.role: The transport-guild boss in the post-town who wrote Tahei off, ~50.
cast.function: The origin faction's ANTAGONIST — abandoned a missing porter to avoid admitting his caravan ran short-weighted/contraband cargo; reachable later as a witness/culprit, tying the origin thread into the same rice-quota racket. Embodies the world's casual disposal of the small.

cast.name: Oharu (the real Tama)
cast.role: The 'spirited-away' child, alive and grown in the down-valley post-town.
cast.function: The living proof whose existence detonates the village's red herring; a girl who ran from a violent stepfather and a near-sale for debt. Reunion kept costly and incomplete (she may not forgive). Connects the lost-child thread to the estate's old rice-debt cover-up.

cast.name: Tedai Kuroiwa
cast.role: The deputy magistrate's agent (tedai) — polite, patient, dangerous.
cast.function: The PRIMARY human antagonist of the regional tiers — architect of the tax-quota cover-up and racket; benefits from the comforting myth; opposes the estate's rise because a rising house asks questions. Defeated by evidence, not violence.

cast.name: Hanzaki
cast.role: A scarred ronin enforcer — the 'one-eyed mountain god' of the pass.
cast.function: Recurring physical threat and the talent-gone-rotten MIRROR — his edge is TRAINED and brutal, NEVER innate; survived by labour-built endurance, never out-talented.

cast.name: Clerk Naozane
cast.role: A guilt-sick junior clerk in the magistrate's office.
cast.function: The crack in the cover-up; supplies documentary proof; a reachable conscience.

cast.name: Rival House Tomita
cast.role: A competing goshi/merchant house that prospered as the Kurosawa fell, partly by exploiting their debt.
cast.function: The inter-house RIVAL on the regional/domain tiers — a status-and-influence competitor to out-maneuver, not kill; embodies talent+luck+ruthlessness against the Kurosawa's earned comeback.

cast.name: The Touring Inspector
cast.role: A senior domain/Edo official.
cast.function: The apex authority reached only at the top tiers — the figure to whom the osso-style petition is made and before whom the house's standing is contested. The gate on the Edo recognition climax; embodies the honoured ceiling (the reachable is reached, the untouchable remains above).

areas.description: Where he wakes, feverish and nameless — the opening 'cell,' one room and one verb (rake the spilled rice). The home of the UI-as-progression engine: the body/rest bar and rice counter live here.
The social threshold where rank is granted and visitors arrive — the diegetic stage for promotions and the Tama-vs-farmhand framing.
The household's heart — the lord, heir, lady, dowager, steward, and staff; dialogue and the rank track live here. The lord's study and ledgers open at the upper rungs.
areas.unlocks_when: Rank 3 (houseman); the study at Rank 6 (steward's deputy).

areas.description: The rice/koku heartbeat — the farming node and the first shinden reclamation site.
Conditioning and idle-combat training under Jubei, opened by the humbling defeat. Combat is the rare exception, gated behind labour-built conditioning.
areas.unlocks_when: Rank 3, after the humbling first fight.

areas.description: Minor labour (hauling, firewood) and the porter's-knot beat. May fold into the forecourt to avoid early reveal fatigue (see open questions).
The smith, the dry-goods/rice broker, the herbalist's stall, the brewer, the weaver — trade and the coin economy reveal; per-shop reputation meters.
areas.unlocks_when: Tier 2 (estate trusts him to run errands; Rank 3+).

areas.description: Smithy, charcoal/carpentry, dyer/weaver — craft chains and apprenticeship side-quests; the craft-guild standing.
Headman Yagoemon's residence — the village reputation roll-up AND the doctored-ledger thread of the lost-child mystery.
Sukezo's inn — the hub for optional, light folklore side-quests (each rumour a yokai tidbit to investigate open-endedly, resolving grounded). NONE gate tier progression.
The soul-calling rite, Priest Ryoa's register of the vanished (a mystery clue), Bon offerings; respectful folk-religion texture.
Where he was found — a roadside jizo; the capped unidentified-hand ambiguity beat (the one residual unresolved, off-screen token).
notable_locations: The offering left by an unknown hand
areas.unlocks_when: Tier 2; the ambiguity beat lingers unresolved.

areas.description: Managed forest/foraging — sansai, mushrooms, herbs, woodcutting, bamboo. The first ring of the danger gradient.
Fishing node + the 'kappa' thread (undertow + smugglers' sinking-spot, physically recoverable evidence) + the weir where he was snagged. A neutral, always-accessible site.
notable_locations: The weir where he was found

areas.description: The 'fox-fire' kiln, hunting range, deeper foraging — the second danger ring.

areas.area: The Forbidden Upstream Ruins of Kuzuhara
areas.region: The Satoyama & Mountains (shared wilderness, Tiers 1-3) / overlaps Origin
areas.description: The drowned upstream hamlet — the protagonist's birthplace, the broken seki (embankment), and the geographic knot where the lost-child evidence, his own origin, and the cover-up physically meet. A re-foundable region node at Tier 3.
areas.unlocks_when: Tier 3 (memory returned + conditioning + standing to range).

areas.description: The lethal terrain where he was found and where his caravan died; the 'one-eyed mountain god' (Hanzaki's lookout); expeditions/logistics.
areas.unlocks_when: Tier 3, top of the conditioning gradient.

areas.description: The post-town (shukuba on the kaido) where Tahei's living family and friends survive — his mother, sister, employer, friends, the guild — and where the grown 'Tama' (Oharu) and clerk Naozane's office sit nearby. The faction-3 support hub; reached via the controlled road (sekisho checkpoints gate it).
areas.unlocks_when: Tier 3 (dream-gated memory + standing to travel the kaido).

areas.description: Multiple villages, trails, and the river network the estate's influence now spans — a logistics/holdings layer, a fresh larger canvas with bigger numbers and no reset.
The daikan's office, the touring Inspector, the formal petition (osso), inter-domain trade, Osaka rice shipping — where the estate's standing is contested at the domain level.
areas.unlocks_when: Tier 4 (distinguished service rendered to the domain).

areas.description: The capital and the climax — indirect, mediated recognition for the restored-and-surpassed house (a famous domain product, the lord's lifted standing via sankin-kotai, a rare merit appointment). NOT personal entry into hatamoto rank or the shogun's presence — the honoured ceiling.
notable_locations: The Edo market where the house's product is celebrated

canonical_s1.endgame: The endgame is RESTORE AND SURPASS, climaxing in indirect recognition at Edo — and it is honoured as historically grounded, not a power fantasy. The protagonist, now the lord's right hand and the architect of the house's revival, completes the influence climb: the Kurosawa, hauled from near-foreclosure, stand restored beyond the grandeur they held three generations ago (before the grandfather Sadamune's failed flood-venture and cover-up broke them). 'Recognition at Edo' is reached as INDIRECT, mediated standing — any of: the house's renowned domain product (built from the shinden/cash-crop/sericulture economy) celebrated in Edo's markets via the sankin-kotai supply chains; the protagonist's labour materially lifting the LORD'S standing during his Edo attendance so the HOUSE gains face at the capital; and/or a rare, hard-won merit commendation or appointment earned by distinguished, documented service. CRUCIALLY, the protagonist does NOT personally become a hatamoto, is NOT received by the shogun, and there is NO rags-to-daimyo arc — the realistic CEILING is itself the emotional thesis made structural: effort takes a nobody and a dying house astonishingly far, then meets a wall the truly powerful built. This mirrors the side-mysteries' PARTIAL JUSTICE (the reachable culprits — Magobei, Yagoemon, Zenroku, eventually Kuroiwa — answer; the untouchable magistrate largely escapes; the osso petition wins partial redress at terrible, deadly risk, never a clean courtroom victory). There is NO reset of any kind: the tier climb IS the ending, and everything built persists. The teaching/management layer that an earlier design routed through a reset is re-homed onto green recruit Tokujiro and recruited origin friends — 'a man who climbed once now starting others from zero.' The epilogue is a tableau of everything built: the restored house seal, the reclaimed fields, the resettled Kuzuhara, the named drowned, the freed and self-determining Oharu, the protagonist's recovered family proud behind him, and a true name (Tahei) and a life he built by hand.
canonical_s1.how_it_paces_unlocks: The estate-rank climb is the dominant driver of the UI-as-progression reveal, with House Influence as the underlying gate-currency, so 'numbers go up' and 'the world enlarges' are ONE motion. SPATIALLY: the player starts trapped in one storehouse room (one verb) and earns the rest of the estate room by room (the rank ladder made of doors), then the village (errands at Rank 3+), then the wilderness (by conditioning), then his own past (by memory), then the region, the castle-town, and finally Edo. Because each tier opens a fresh, larger canvas with bigger numbers and NO reset, the map physically embodies the tier escalation — influence is something you can SEE growing as the world you can reach grows. SYSTEMICALLY: each rank-up fires through the universal rewards/unlock bus as ONE event that simultaneously pushes a diegetic log line, reveals the next panel/tab/resource/area, grants the perk, and advances a story flag — so feature unlocks read as plot, never silent menu growth. Concretely: Rank 0 = body/rest bar + rice counter; Rank 1 = labour loop + paddies + koku; Rank 2 = Skills tab + foraging/woodcutting; Rank 3 = main house + village tier + the drill yard / idle-combat (after the humbling fight); Rank 4 = the assignment/management panel + labour-gang; Rank 5 = the House Influence macro-resource made visible + cash-crop/proto-industry levers; Rank 6 = the ledgers + the tier-expansion map; Rank 7 (capstone) = the estate gate to the wider world and the bridge to the higher tiers. Every reveal carries an Influence-band (plus flag/skill) predicate, so the macro spine literally paces the UI. The three faction tracks differ in SHAPE to keep the pacing varied: the estate's steep geometric rank gates space the big promotions (interleaved with smaller responsibility/perk drips so the climb has texture between gates, never letting the next goal exceed ~2-3x the prior); the village's gentle per-node meters give frequent small dopamine; the origin's discrete memory milestones are chunky one-time unlocks on a guaranteed cadence (foreshadowed for hours before opening so its absence reads as anticipation). The side factions act as Influence MULTIPLIERS (tuned so weaving them in roughly halves time-to-next-tier — felt, never a wall), so a curious player paces faster and a spine-only player still finishes, just slower and lonelier. The folklore rumours stay strictly optional and never gate tier progression. Everything is data-driven (areas/panels/resources as registries with unlock predicates over GameState), deterministic under the one seeded RNG, with balance/unlock tables generated into docs/ and headlessly regression-testable via the DEV play API (assert each rank/standing/tier gate fires at the intended state).

decisions_to_lock.question: Reverse ADR D-004 (one diegetic season-cycle reset) in favour of the locked no-reset tier escalation?
options: Reverse D-004: tier climb (estate->village->region->domain->Edo) replaces prestige; NO reset of any kind
decisions_to_lock.recommendation: Reverse D-004 with a new superseding ADR; mark D-004 ⛔ REVERSED.
decisions_to_lock.why: The locked direction explicitly replaces prestige with the tier climb and states 'NO RESET OF ANY KIND.' The decision log is authoritative and still encodes the reset; building faction/tier/area designs against a stale ADR creates live contradictions. The 'meta-progress carries forward' guardrail is trivially satisfied since nothing ever resets. This changes what the game is at the macro level, so it must be locked with the human per CLAUDE.md.

decisions_to_lock.question: Broaden the origin faction (faction 3) from 'family dead / drowned hamlet' to LIVING family & friends in a post-town?
options: Keep the original 'orphaned, family dead, drowned Kuzuhara' canon
Broaden to surviving kin (mother, sister), old employer, friends, sweetheart, and guild-mates in Magome-juku, with Kuzuhara as a re-foundable region node
decisions_to_lock.recommendation: Broaden to living family/friends; keep a bittersweet edge (he was written off; some won't easily forgive).
decisions_to_lock.why: The locked direction requires faction 3 to be people who SUPPORT his estate achievements with pride, allies, and trade ties. Without living people there is no one to reconnect with — a direct contradiction. This supersedes the spine's family-death framing and needs a human nod since it touches canon. Resolves the 'found stranger -> believable rise' and 'who supports him' tensions.

decisions_to_lock.question: Frame 'recognition at Edo' as indirect/mediated (the honoured ceiling), explicitly capping personal rank?
options: Indirect recognition: famous domain product + lord's lifted standing + rare merit appointment; the protagonist never personally becomes hatamoto
decisions_to_lock.recommendation: Indirect/mediated recognition with an explicit, honoured ceiling.
decisions_to_lock.why: A lone rural goshi house reaching personal hatamoto rank in one working life would collapse the grounded, no-magic, partial-justice promise into power-fantasy. Routing the climb through authentic levers (shinden, meibutsu, goyokin, adoption/marriage, distinguished service) keeps the BIG fantasy intact and historically defensible, and makes the ceiling the thesis made structural. May warrant its own ADR since it touches the locked ambition.

decisions_to_lock.question: How many estate ranks at the local tier, and do higher tiers get their own rank ladders?
options: Eight discrete ranks (stray -> chief steward), higher tiers track Influence not personal rank
Each tier gets a fresh rank ladder
decisions_to_lock.recommendation: Eight ranks at the local tier (as specified); higher tiers track House Influence and outward expansion rather than a fresh personal rank ladder.
decisions_to_lock.why: Eight gives a legible, well-paced climb to 'the top a non-born man can plausibly reach,' with the capstone as the tier bridge. Re-laddering every tier would balloon scope and dilute the sense of having reached the personal summit. Exact count is a balance/pacing call to confirm with the human.

decisions_to_lock.question: How large is the side-faction (village + origin) multiplier on Influence / tier-climb speed?
options: Moderate (~halves time-to-next-tier when both woven in)
decisions_to_lock.recommendation: Moderate — tuned so weaving both in roughly halves time-to-next-tier; felt, never a hard wall.
decisions_to_lock.why: The side factions must MATTER to the spine (so they aren't pointless) without ever gating it (so spine-only stays viable). The exact multiplier needs the pacing playtest the mechanics work already flags; lock the design intent now, tune the number later.

decisions_to_lock.question: What is the v1 launch scope across the five tiers?
options: Tiers 1-2 complete, Tier 3 as a stubbed cliff-hanger
A thin vertical slice through all five tiers
decisions_to_lock.recommendation: Tiers 1-2 (estate + village) complete and Tier 3 stubbed as the cliff-hanger; Tiers 4-5 as roadmap.
decisions_to_lock.why: Five full canvases is an unshippable saga for v1; the user prefers starting lean and adding back deliberately. A complete estate+village slice with a Tier-3 hook delivers the core perseverance fantasy and the UI-reveal signature while keeping scope sane. This is a §7-roadmap decision the process gates on human review.

open_questions: Tier count and naming: are exactly five tiers (estate/village/region/domain/Edo) right, or should 'region' and 'domain' merge, or a 'province' tier be added? Needs a balance/pacing pass.
Exact House Influence band thresholds and conversion-ledger weights (how much Influence a shinden plot vs. a paid debt vs. a won petition is worth) — defer to the balance facet/§4.
Does Influence ever decay or only stall? (Recommend: never decays passively; a small set of scripted, recoverable dents only.) Confirm against the 'no wipe' feel.
Does the koku/Influence economy reach magnitudes across five tiers that need scientific/abbreviated big-number formatting? Carried from the mechanics brainstorm.

Pacing lock: should the side-mysteries (lost-child, origin) and the tier climb climax together at Tier 5/Edo, or do the mysteries resolve around Tier 3-4 (Region/Domain) with Tier 5 as a quieter restoration victory lap? Affects narrative pacing.
Is the Tama-vs-farmhand lean a continuous slider or a small set of discrete leanings (Tama/neutral/farmhand), and can it be freely re-swung or does it harden after key beats?
Does the estate ACTIVELY penalise high village standing (jealousy/split-loyalty) as a live mechanic, or is that tension purely narrative flavour?
Where exactly on the memory/tier timeline does the origin track unlock, and what is the exact dream cadence cap (in-game days or skill/standing threshold)?
Do village allies seconded to the estate count against village production (a real loan with a cost) or are they free helpers?

Should the estate's top role be a karo-equivalent 'chief steward' or a humbler yonin/steward title, given a goshi house arguably doesn't warrant a karo? Needs an authenticity-vs-legibility call on title vocabulary.

Which decade/sub-period and domain archetype is the canonical setting? This sharpens the rise-levers, the meibutsu product, and the goshi-treatment model (keep fictional/generic-rural but pick a template).

Should adoption/marriage-up be an actual late-game mechanic (the most authentic top-rung lever) or stay narrative flavour, given it introduces relationship/family-line systems that may exceed v1 scope?

Whose neck is on the line in the osso petition — the protagonist, an ally, or a gimin-style martyr NPC? The most authentic and affecting option is real personal risk; needs a narrative decision consistent with the partial-justice guardrail.
How granular is the estate room-unlock — six separate reveals, or fold stables/woodlot into the forecourt to avoid early reveal fatigue?
Do the macro-tiers (region/domain/Edo) get full sub-area maps or more abstract 'holdings/influence' boards over a thinner location set? Decides late-game spatial authoring load.
How many inn rumour-quests ship, and confirm the hard rule that ALL are optional and NONE gate tier progression.

Confirm canonical place/house names: estate = Kurosawa, valley = Asagiri-dani, origin post-town = Magome-juku; and resolve any remaining name collisions (the sister renamed Okimi to avoid the superseded survivor 'Kiku'; confirm whether any old-canon Kiku material is salvaged into the Kuzuhara/origin thread).

Does the late true-name reveal (Tahei) and its timing survive the reframe now that identity is explicitly a side thread, and is it softened/optional rather than a spine climax? Needs reconciling with ADR D-006.

violations.constraint: Establish reversing ADRs before treating reversed decisions as locked canon (CLAUDE.md: lock direction-changing decisions with the human as ADRs; PRD §1 is marked 'Locked (ADRs D-001…D-006)').
violations.problem: The canonical §1 asserts as settled fact four things that DIRECTLY CONTRADICT the still-live locked ADRs, while its OWN decisions_to_lock array admits they are not yet locked. The decision log ends at D-006; there is no D-007+. (1) D-004 ('one grounded diegetic late-game RESET') is still ✅ Decided and NOT marked ⛔ REVERSED, yet §1 asserts 'NO reset of any kind' throughout and even has decisions_to_lock[0] requesting the reversal. (2) The locked spine/PRD origin canon is 'family dead / drowned Kuzuhara hamlet'; §1 broadens to LIVING family in a post-town, again admitted as an unlocked ask (decisions_to_lock[1]). (3) The indirect/capped Edo ceiling is requested in decisions_to_lock[2], not locked. (4) D-006 fixes the true-name reveal at 'Act 4' as an authored climax; §1 de-emphasises it to a 'late memory beat' that is 'no longer the spine's climax.' Publishing this JSON as 'CANONICAL §1' while the authoritative ADR log still encodes the opposite creates live, shipping contradictions. Fix: do not call this canonical until superseding ADRs (reverse D-004; broaden origin; cap the Edo ceiling; reconfirm/soften D-006) are written and the human signs off, per CLAUDE.md's stop-and-ask rule for decisions that change what the game is.

violations.constraint: Keep the setting generic-rural / fictional, NOT pinned to a real named place (established authenticity guardrail in the grounded-story-spine authenticity notes: 'keep it generic-rural, not pinned to a real named disaster/place').
violations.problem: §1 names the origin post-town 'Magome-juku' (and repeatedly 'the kaido'). Magome-juku (馬籠宿, Magome-shuku) is a REAL, very famous Nakasendō post-town in the Kiso valley. Pinning the otherwise-fictional world (fictional Kurosawa estate, Asagiri valley, Kuzuhara hamlet) to a real, identifiable shukuba contradicts the established generic-rural guideline, invites tourist-brochure expectations, and risks geographic incoherence (Magome sits on the Nakasendō in mountainous Kiso/Mino — does the invented Asagiri valley + drowned Kuzuhara actually fit there?). The open_questions even flag 'confirm canonical place/house names,' showing this was never settled. Fix: rename the post-town to an invented shukuba (e.g. a fictional '-juku' on an unnamed kaidō) and keep the road generic, OR get an explicit ADR consciously adopting a real-place setting and then research-harden the geography. Do not silently canonize a real place against the standing guideline.

violations.constraint: Folklore is believed-atmosphere only; residual ambiguity hard-capped at ≤1 unresolved token, mundane-readable (D-002).
violations.problem: §1's folklore_handling says residual ambiguity is 'hard-capped at ~1-2 unresolved... beats.' D-002's consequences lock the cap at '≤1 unresolved token per playthrough.' '~1-2' silently loosens a locked numeric guardrail (and the '~' makes it fuzzier still). Minor but it is a drift from a locked number. Fix: state '≤1' to match D-002, or write an ADR if the cap is being intentionally raised.

weaknesses: PROTAGONIST AGE DRIFT (un-flagged): The locked PRD/spine and D-003 repeatedly specify a 'mediocre 17-year-old.' §1 silently drops the age and substitutes 'young man' everywhere. This is arguably an improvement (a wholly self-made adult rise reads better than a 17-year-old becoming a karo-equivalent), and 'young man' is safer for the Ohana sweetheart thread and the porter backstory. But it is an unacknowledged change to locked text and should be made explicit (pick and state an age band, e.g. ~18-20) rather than left vague, so dialogue/art authoring downstream is consistent.

TITLE-CEILING TENSION (authenticity, partially self-aware): §1 has the protagonist reach 'chief steward / the lord's right hand (karo-kaku)' and even a 'symbolic adoption into the house name.' For a SMALL, threadbare GOSHI house this is on the edge of plausibility: goshi were rural samurai of modest means; a true 'karo' (家老) is a domain/daimyo-house elder, far above a single hill estate. §1's own rank label hedges with 'karo-KAKU' (karo-equivalent) and open_questions flags exactly this ('a goshi house arguably doesn't warrant a karo... authenticity-vs-legibility call'), which is good self-awareness — but the body text and cast (Genemon 'the karo-equivalent chief steward') still lean on the grand word. Recommend resolving toward a humbler, accurate title (yonin/家令/家扶 'house steward,' or simply 'the steward / the lord's right hand') and reserving 'karo' language for narration-as-aspiration only. The 'merit-elevation or symbolic adoption' top rung is the most authenticity-sensitive single claim and needs the §4/§5 research-harden the doc already promises.

EDO-RECOGNITION GROUNDING (mostly handled, one soft spot): §1's capped, indirect Edo recognition (famous meibutsu via sankin-kotai supply chains; the lord's lifted standing; a rare merit commendation; explicitly NOT personal hatamoto status / shogunal audience) is historically defensible and the partial-justice ceiling is a genuine strength. Soft spot: the speed. A near-foreclosure goshi house climbing five tiers to nationally-celebrated Edo meibutsu and a merit appointment 'in one working life' is a compressed but not impossible arc IF the in-fiction timescale is decades, not a couple of seasons. The doc never states the elapsed in-world time of the full climb; without a multi-decade span the rise reads as implausibly fast. Recommend §1 commit to a generational/decadal timescale for the upper tiers so 'restored AND surpassed three-generations-ago grandeur' is earned, not asserted.

RESET RESIDUE / D-004 TEACHING-LAYER RE-HOMING is handled well but leaves a faint scar: §1 correctly re-homes the old reset's teaching/management idea onto Tokujiro and recruited origin friends ('a man who climbed once now starting others from zero'). Good. But because D-004 is not yet formally reversed, both the 'no reset' claim and the salvaged teaching layer sit on an unlocked foundation (see violation 1). Once the ADR lands this becomes a strength; until then it is contingent.

INFLUENCE-AS-IDENTITY-SPINE CHECK (passes, worth stating): The reviewer's core worry — that place identity/origin creeps back to being THE SPINE — is NOT realized here. §1 is disciplined: the estate-rise is named the spine in the vision, all six pillars, premise, interrelation, and how_it_paces_unlocks; identity and origin are repeatedly and explicitly demoted to side threads that 'pay off only in feeling, allies, and flavour, never power' and 'feed the spine without ever gating it.' The true-name reveal is explicitly de-emphasised. This is a strength, not a violation; noting it so the human can see the constraint was met.
fixes: Do NOT publish this JSON as 'CANONICAL §1' yet. First write the four superseding ADRs the doc's own decisions_to_lock array calls for, and get the human's sign-off (CLAUDE.md gates direction-changing decisions on human review): D-007 reverse D-004 (mark D-004 '⛔ REVERSED by D-007'; strike its claim) → tier-climb replaces reset, NO reset of any kind; D-008 broaden origin to living family/friends in a post-town with Kuzuhara as a re-foundable node; D-009 cap Edo recognition as indirect/mediated (honoured ceiling, no personal hatamoto/shogunal audience); D-010 reconfirm D-006 but soften the true-name reveal from spine-climax to de-emphasised side beat. Update prd.md §1 in place to match (it currently still describes the 17-year-old, the reset, and family-dead canon).

Rename 'Magome-juku' to an INVENTED post-town on an unnamed/fictional kaidō (e.g. '<invented>-juku'), restoring the generic-rural guardrail — OR, if a real-place setting is genuinely wanted, write an explicit ADR adopting it and then research-harden whether the invented Asagiri valley + drowned Kuzuhara hamlet + the post-town are geographically coherent on that real road. Resolve open_question on canonical place/house names at the same time.
Tighten the residual-ambiguity cap in folklore_handling from '~1-2' to '≤1 unresolved token per playthrough' to match D-002 exactly (or ADR the change).
State the protagonist's age band explicitly (recommend ~18-20, a self-made young adult) instead of the vague 'young man,' and update D-006/§1 so the age change is acknowledged, not silent.

Resolve the estate top-title authenticity question now rather than deferring: prefer a humble, accurate steward title (yonin / kasai / 'house steward / the lord's right hand') and demote 'karo'/'karo-kaku' to in-fiction aspirational narration only; drop or carefully caveat the 'symbolic adoption into the house name' so the top rung stays historically defensible for a modest goshi house. Flag this explicitly for the §4/§5 research-harden the doc already promises.

Commit §1 to a generational/decadal in-world timescale for the upper-tier climb (Tier 3→5), so the 'restore AND surpass three-generations-ago grandeur' arc and the famous-meibutsu-at-Edo outcome read as earned over decades, not compressed into a few seasons.
authenticity_notes: NO OVERT MAGIC / NO PLAYER MAGIC: PASS. §1 is rigorous — every belief resolves 1:1 to a human/natural cause; the kamikakushi master-belief is framed as grief-coping over a human truth (a child fleeing debt/violence); 'never confirmed magic, never player magic' is stated repeatedly; the 'helpful fox' is explicitly de-fanged; the one residual ambiguity is mundane-readable. Only nit is the ≤1 vs ~1-2 cap drift (above).

NO HIDDEN EDGE / SECRETLY-SPECIAL PROTAGONIST: PASS, and notably strict. The porter's-knot is correctly framed as procedural body-memory = NARRATIVE IDENTITY CLUE ONLY, zero mechanical bonus, with the source (Denbei taught it) made mundane. The dream is bound to MEMORY-ONLY (only things he actually lived; the 'voice in the water' is a real recalled person, not a spirit), confers ACCESS not power, and is headlessly regression-testable (assert a fragment fired AND no stat/recipe granted). Pillar 4 and the no-hidden-edge guardrails explicitly forbid bloodline/secret training/a body that 'remembers' combat. The talent-foils (Hanzaki, Naoyuki, Kanbei, Tomita) are all required to be TRAINED/lucky/ruthless, never innately gifted — effort-over-talent thesis intact. The 'returned Tama' specialness is explicitly FALSE. This is the strongest-honoured constraint in the doc.

IDENTITY AS SIDE THREAD, NOT SPINE: PASS. Estate-rise is named the spine everywhere; identity/origin are explicitly demoted and explicitly NEVER gate the spine. The Tama-vs-farmhand lean only shifts RATES and FLAVOUR, never availability, with ambivalence a valid in-character stance — matches the locked 'roleplay not character-creator' decision and the fixed-male/no-rename rule (D-006). The fixed-male requirement is correctly load-bearing for the gender-drift clue (the real Tama was a girl mis-remembered as a boy).

THREE-FACTION STRUCTURE & TIERS-REPLACE-PRESTIGE: PASS on structure. Three factions are deliberately distinct in SHAPE (estate = gated rank ladder; village = continuous multi-node web; origin = discrete memory-milestone support track) and coupled by separate currencies feeding one House Influence macro-resource — a genuinely good anti-'one bar painted three colours' design.
