Tiers (estate→village→region→domain→Edo) replace prestige. The ONLY problem is that 'tiers replace prestige / no reset' isn't yet locked because D-004 still stands (see violation 1).

ESTATE-RESTORATION AS MAIN QUEST: PASS. The main quest is unambiguously the estate climb + outward influence expansion; side factions enrich but never replace it; the spine is completable spine-only (just slower/lonelier). No drift detected.

VILLAGE-CHIEF vs SHŌYA RECONCILIATION: PASS / CONSISTENT. §1 reconciles them correctly: Yagoemon is 'historically the SHOYA/NANUSHI headman... drawn from the local elite and assisted by the village's self-governing body (kumigashira, hyakushodai) under murauke.' This matches the PRD (Yagōemon = shōya/nanushi) and the spine's authenticity notes (shōya doctoring ledgers is well-attested). The murauke (village-contract tax) and kumigashira/hyakushōdai self-government detail is accurate and a nice authenticity win. Yagoemon's civic-double-duty (warm quest-giver → reachable frightened-middle-man culprit, 'never a monster') is consistent with the locked partial-justice framing. Minor romanization inconsistency only: PRD uses 'Yagōemon', JSON uses 'Yagoemon' — pick one (macron policy) project-wide.

FOLKLORE NOT TREATED AS LITERALLY REAL: PASS. Folklore is light flavour via the inn rumours board; named yokai are optional tidbits, none gate progression; the campaign is explicitly NOT built around yokai. Shinto vs Buddhist texture is correctly distinguished (shimenawa/boundary-mountain kami/soul-calling rite vs roadside jizo as guardian of children & travellers, Bon offerings, the temple death-register as hard evidence) rather than blurred into generic 'Shinto magic' — a real authenticity strength. 'No rite ever mechanically works' is stated.

CULTURAL SENSITIVITY: PASS. Dark material (a child nearly sold for debt, a drowned hamlet, a covered-up disaster) is handled off-screen with restraint and counter-weighted by found-family warmth; kamikakushi is treated as grief-coping, not superstition to mock; Oharu/the real Tama keeps agency (may not forgive; freed and self-determining). Inherits the spine's good instinct that disaster survivors have 'dignity and agency, never wild people to be tamed.' osso is correctly flagged as genuinely deadly (the partial-justice thesis) rather than a clean courtroom win — historically honest and the most sensitivity-critical mechanic, handled well.

RISE-PLAUSIBILITY: MOSTLY GROUNDED via authentic Edo upward-mobility levers (shinden reclamation as the canonical goshi-advancement lever; cash crops/sericulture/sake/textile by-employments; river/road works as distinguished service; debt restructuring from usury to merchant/domain finance; goyokin contributions + adoption/marriage-up; meibutsu reaching Osaka then Edo via sankin-kotai). These are real and well-chosen. Two grounding caveats remain (in weaknesses): the timescale must be decadal/generational for the climb to be plausible, and the very top rung (karo-kaku title + symbolic adoption) is on the edge for a modest goshi house and needs the promised research-harden.
verify.verdict: FAILS as 'canonical' — not on creative substance (the story design is strong and honours the hard constraints well) but on PROCESS and two concrete authenticity points. The biggest problem is that this JSON declares itself CANONICAL §1 while contradicting the still-live locked ADRs: D-004 (the single late-game reset) has NOT been reversed, the origin-broadening and the capped Edo ceiling are NOT locked, and the true-name de-emphasis re-opens D-006 — all four are even admitted as unlocked in the doc's own decisions_to_lock array, yet asserted as settled in the body. Per CLAUDE.md these are direction-changing calls that must be locked with the human as superseding ADRs FIRST; until then prd.md §1 still says the opposite (17-year-old, a reset, family-dead). Second, it pins the deliberately-fictional world to a REAL famous place — 'Magome-juku' on the Nakasendō — breaking the established generic-rural guardrail. Smaller drifts: the residual-ambiguity cap loosened from ≤1 to ~1-2; the protagonist age silently dropped; the 'karo-kaku' top title and 'symbolic adoption' are at the plausibility edge for a small goshi house; and the multi-tier rise needs an explicit decadal timescale to read as earned. CREDIT WHERE DUE: the no-magic / no-player-magic, no-hidden-edge, identity-as-side-thread, three-distinct-faction-shapes, shōya-headman, folklore-as-light-flavour, and cultural-sensitivity constraints are all honoured carefully and in several places exceeded. Fix the ADR/process gap, the real-place name, and the small numeric/title/timescale drifts, and this is shippable canon."

facets.facet: Facet A — Estate-restoration main-quest arc & influence tiers (the macro spine)
facets.summary: The macro spine of Kamikakushi is a five-tier influence climb that REPLACES prestige entirely: a declining goshi (country-samurai) house, the Kurosawa estate, is hauled from near-foreclosure to standing at Edo, and the protagonist rises from a taken-in stray to the man who runs its expansion. The single tracked macro-resource is HOUSE INFLUENCE (家威, "ka-i") — the estate's standing as the wider world reckons it — fed by but distinct from koku/coin. Influence is earned by converting concrete achievements (reclaimed land, paid-down debt, profitable trade, distinguished service, named allies, won petitions) into recognized standing, and it is the gate-currency for the whole UI reveal: each tier opens a fresh, larger canvas of systems and an order-of-magnitude bigger number range. The five tiers — ESTATE -> VILLAGE -> REGION -> DOMAIN/CASTLE-TOWN -> EDO — each have a theme, an entry gate, headline main-quest beats, and a visible "the estate grew" payoff (land, retainers, buildings, trade routes, formal title). The protagonist's three-faction web feeds the spine: the estate IS the spine; the village is the first expansion frontier (Tier 2); and his ORIGIN TOWN (faction 3 / Kuzuhara + its post-town) becomes a concrete Region-tier expansion target and trade ally, fusing the personal memory thread into the macro climb. Every rise mechanism is a real Edo upward-mobility lever (shinden reclamation, cash crops, sericulture, sake/textiles, river/road works, debt restructuring via merchant finance, distinguished service to the domain, patronage, strategic marriage/adoption), and the climax — the omemie (audience) right and recognition at Edo — is a historically real status threshold, not a fantasy.

key_designs.name: House Influence (家威, ka-i) — the single tracked macro-resource
key_designs.description: Influence is the estate's RECOGNIZED STANDING in the eyes of progressively wider authorities, and it is the one number the whole game's UI-reveal is gated on. It is deliberately NOT koku and NOT coin: koku/coin are the inputs you spend and grind; Influence is the cumulative, mostly-monotonic score of what the estate has visibly become. It accrues only when a concrete achievement is RECOGNIZED by the relevant authority for the current tier — e.g. reclaiming a paddy adds koku immediately but only converts to Influence when the village/steward/domain acknowledges it (a recorded yield, a granted title, a sealed contract). This keeps Influence diegetic ('the books now show...') and keeps it the gate-currency: every panel/tab/system/area unlock in the incremental reveal has an Influence (plus flag/skill) predicate, so the macro spine literally paces the UI. Influence has named BANDS that map 1:1 to the five tiers; crossing a band is the tier-up moment. It never resets (locked: no prestige). It can stall (a tier-gate you haven't met) but a few beats can dent it (a debt called, a scandal) — small, recoverable, never a wipe — to keep tension.

key_designs.name: The five influence tiers: theme, entry gate, headline beats, visible growth
key_designs.description: TIER 1 ESTATE (theme: earn your keep & a place at the table; canvas: the household, one valley estate; gate IN: survive convalescence + first labour, already met). Headline beats: be taken in; become a trusted hand; discover the house is quietly insolvent (a merchant loan/goyokin coming due, fields fallow, retainers down to old Jubei); the protagonist proposes/begins the first shinden reclamation. Visible growth: fallow plots return to paddy, the storehouse fills, a second hand is hired. UI payoff: the estate dashboard fully blooms (labour, koku, household roster). | TIER 2 VILLAGE (theme: the estate as a presence in its own valley; canvas: the nearby village(s), shops, craftsmen, the headman; gate IN: first reclamation recorded + the house solvent enough to extend credit rather than beg it). Headline beats: the estate starts buying village output and contracting craftsmen; cash crops & proto-industry come online (sericulture, sake, textiles); a river/ford works project; building reputation with Headman Yagoemon; the estate becomes the valley's economic anchor. Visible growth: new buildings (a kura/storehouse, a workshop, a silk shed), the estate's mon appearing on village contracts, the first proper retainers. | TIER 3 REGION (theme: reach beyond the home valley; canvas: a cluster of valleys, upstream Kuzuhara ruins, the down-valley post-town, roads & sekisho; gate IN: village anchored + a tradeable surplus + roads/porters under estate control). Headline beats: open trade routes along the kaido; RE-FOUND KUZUHARA as a resettlement (this is the faction-3 fusion — the protagonist's origin town becomes a Region expansion node, its returning people his allies and a labour/trade base); secure the embankment so the disaster cannot recur; the estate's name travels. Visible growth: a regional map of nodes (villages, post-town, Kuzuhara) feeding the estate; pack-trains, a second estate compound or branch holding. | TIER 4 DOMAIN / CASTLE-TOWN (theme: be reckoned with by the people who actually rule; canvas: the castle town, the domain's officials, inter-han markets, Osaka rice shipping; gate IN: distinguished SERVICE to the domain — flood-control, surplus rice in a lean year, or the petition/case (osso) resolving in the house's favour). Headline beats: invited (warily) into domain affairs; restructure the old debt through proper merchant finance / a daimyo loan rather than usury; a strategic marriage or adoption alliance lifts the house's rank; the estate's goods reach Osaka. Visible growth: a townhouse, a granted increase in formal kokudaka/standing, the steward installed at the castle town, the house name on inter-han ledgers. | TIER 5 EDO (theme: recognition at the capital — restore AND surpass the 2-3-generations-ago grandeur; canvas: Edo, the bakufu status ladder, the omemie threshold; gate IN: domain sponsorship + enough standing to be presented). Headline beats: the house is presented at Edo; the climax is winning the OMEMIE right (audience) — the historically real line between gokenin-grade and audience-grade status — i.e. real recognition; the protagonist, now the house's architect, secures a name and standing it has not held in three generations. Visible growth: the Edo presentation set-piece, the restored-and-surpassed house seal, an epilogue tableau of everything built. The tier climb IS the ending; there is no reset after.

key_designs.name: Influence-conversion ledger: the rise mechanisms as authentic Edo levers
key_designs.description: Each tier exposes a menu of historically grounded ways to CONVERT grind into Influence, so 'numbers go up' always reads as a real path a small declining goshi house could climb: (1) SHINDEN land reclamation — clearing/draining new paddy, the canonical real goshi-advancement lever (a granted increase in recognized yield is the purest Influence source); (2) cash crops & proto-industry — sericulture (silk), sake brewing, textile weaving as village by-employments the estate organizes and takes a cut of; (3) river & road works — fords, embankments, kaido upkeep that both protect yield and earn official credit (distinguished service); (4) debt restructuring — moving from a strangling usurious loan to proper merchant finance / a domain loan, turning a liability into solvency and then into creditworthiness (the estate eventually extends credit instead of begging it); (5) distinguished SERVICE to the domain — relief-rice in a famine, flood control, the petition that exposes the cover-up, all earning official standing; (6) patronage & alliance — strategic marriage and the very real Edo practice of merchant/farmer buy-in via ADOPTION into samurai rank, plus patrons who vouch for the house upward; (7) trade ties — opening routes to the post-town and ORIGIN town and onward to the inter-han / Osaka market. Each lever is a small economic sub-loop (spend koku/coin/labour -> produce -> get recognized -> Influence), and which levers are AVAILABLE is itself part of the tier reveal.

key_designs.name: Faction-3 fusion: the origin town as a macro expansion node
key_designs.description: The locked direction requires the protagonist's ORIGIN (faction 3) to feed the spine, not sit beside it. Design: Kuzuhara (his drowned upstream home hamlet) and the down-valley post-town where the grown Tama/Oharu and his old contacts live are made CONCRETE region-tier nodes. As his memory returns (the personal/side thread), he unlocks the ability to re-engage them; their pride in his estate achievements converts into hard macro assets — returning Kuzuhara settlers as a labour/producer base, the post-town as a trade partner and information broker (Sokichi the peddler), old caravan/porter ties as ready-made trade routes plugging into Tier 3 route-opening. The embankment that killed his caravan becomes a Tier-3 river-works Influence project that also resolves his backstory. This makes the personal thread PAY the spine: recovering who he was literally expands the house. Crucially, per the locked constraints, returning memory grants ZERO mechanical bonus — what it grants is ACCESS (new nodes/allies/quests unlock narratively), and the assets must still be built up by grind like everything else.

key_designs.name: Tier gates as diegetic thresholds, not score walls
key_designs.description: Each tier-up is gated by a concrete, in-world achievement (not merely 'reach N Influence'), so the climb reads as plot: Estate->Village = first shinden recorded + house solvent; Village->Region = village anchored + tradeable surplus + roads under control; Region->Domain = a distinguished SERVICE rendered to the domain (the petition/osso resolving, or famine relief, or flood control); Domain->Edo = domain sponsorship + presentable standing. The historical sekisho (checkpoints) and the omemie audience-right are used as literal, authentic gates: you cannot freely move goods/people across domain boundaries or be 'seen' at the capital without the standing that the tier represents. This gives the gates teeth and authenticity while keeping the Influence band as the underlying tunable measure.

key_designs.name: Reconciling with locked PRD canon (act structure & the former 'reset')
key_designs.description: The existing PRD §1 (locked ADRs D-001..D-006) describes a six-act story whose Act 5 was a single diegetic season-cycle RESET carrying meta-progress. The current locked direction REPLACES that reset with the tier escalation. Recommended reconciliation: the acts and the tiers are largely COMPATIBLE — Acts 0-1 sit inside Tier 1 (Estate), Act 2 spans late Tier 1 / early Tier 2, Acts 3-4 (investigation, the case, Kuzuhara) ARE the Tier 2->Region->Domain climb, and the old 'Act 5 reset' is reframed as the Region->Domain->Edo continuation rather than a wipe. The 'meta-progress carries forward' guardrail is now trivially satisfied because there is no reset at all: everything simply persists as the canvas grows. This needs one new ADR superseding the reset framing (see open questions); it does NOT touch the grounded/no-magic, mediocre-start, or folklore ADRs.

facets.how_it_serves_the_vision: The locked vision is a perseverance fantasy at GRAND scale: a mediocre nobody and a dying house both rise from nothing, by toil, to recognition at Edo — and the signature feature is that the UI itself unlocks as you progress. House Influence as the single gate-currency makes those two things the SAME thing: the estate's climb literally paces the reveal of every panel, system, and area, so the player experiences 'numbers go up' AND 'the world enlarges' as one motion. The five tiers replace prestige with genuine ESCALATION (each tier is a bigger canvas and an order-of-magnitude bigger number range) so the long game keeps opening outward instead of resetting — honoring the locked 'no reset of any kind' and 'tier climb IS the escalation' constraints. It delivers the ambition exactly as specified: restore AND surpass the 2-3-generations grandeur, climaxing with real (historically grounded) standing at Edo via the omemie threshold. And it keeps the protagonist's growth-by-grind dignity central — Influence is bought with the same earned sweat as every skill rank, never gifted by bloodline or returning memory.
facets.ties_to_mechanics: Maps cleanly onto the locked pure-core architecture. Influence is one more resource in core/state's GameState (a monotonic-ish counter plus a band index), produced by the same recognized-achievement events that the rewards/unlock bus already emits — so it needs no new subsystem, just a conversion ledger (achievement -> Influence) and a band table. The UI-reveal engine already specifies every panel/tab/resource/area as DATA with an unlock predicate over game state; tiers slot in as the top-level predicate dimension (each reveal gets a tier/Influence-band condition), making 'each tier opens a fresh canvas' a content-table change, not view logic. The economic levers (shinden, cash crops, sericulture, sake/textiles, trade routes) are producer/upgrade loops on the existing koku-spine pattern (cost = base*1.15^owned, ~5x tier jumps), each new lever lighting its own panel on first unlock. Tier gates are flag/quest predicates the dialogue+quest bus already grants. Everything stays deterministic under the one seeded RNG, and the per-faction reputation system (estate/village/origin) feeds tier gates directly. Single-source-of-truth holds: the tier table, Influence band thresholds, and conversion ledger generate the balance/progression docs in docs/.
risks: Number-range / balance blowup: five tiers each an order of magnitude larger risks either trivializing earlier grind or requiring big-number formatting and careful curve tuning the existing brainstorm already flagged as an open question (does the koku economy even reach magnitudes needing it?). Tier count and Influence band thresholds must be tuned against the real play length.

Scope explosion: each tier 'opens a fresh, larger canvas of systems' — five full canvases is a lot of content for a v1. Risk of an unshippable saga. Mitigation: define a v1 vertical slice (likely Tiers 1-2 fully, Tier 3 stubbed) and let later tiers be roadmap, not launch.

Pacing mismatch with story length: the brainstorm pegs the story at ~2-4 weeks of play, but a five-tier estate-empire climb to Edo implies a much longer idle arc. The macro spine and the authored 6-act mystery must be paced to END together, or one will badly outrun the other (the mystery resolves at Tier ~4 while Edo is Tier 5).

'Restore the estate' could drift into pure city-builder/4X tedium and dilute the grounded character story and the cozy daily-labour texture that are the locked emotional core. Influence-as-spreadsheet must stay diegetic and story-framed.

Reconciliation debt: the locked PRD/ADRs still encode the Act-5 RESET. Until a superseding ADR is written, there is a live contradiction between canon docs and this facet that could confuse downstream facet/synthesis work.

Authenticity overreach: a single small goshi house reaching genuine Edo/omemie recognition in one protagonist's working life is at the optimistic edge of plausibility. It is defensible (merchant/farmer buy-in via adoption, distinguished service, kokudaka increases all real) but must be paced over years and framed as exceptional, or it undercuts the grounded promise.

Faction-3 fusion vs the no-mechanical-bonus rule: making the origin town a macro asset risks implying returning memory 'pays off' mechanically. Must be authored as ACCESS-only (unlocks nodes/quests), with the assets still grind-built, to stay inside D-003.
open_questions: Tier count and naming: are exactly five tiers (estate/village/region/domain/Edo) right, or should 'region' and 'domain' merge, or an extra 'province' tier exist? Needs a balance/pacing pass.
v1 scope: which tiers ship at launch vs roadmap? (Recommend Tiers 1-2 complete, Tier 3 as the cliff-hanger.) This is a §7-roadmap decision the locked process gates on human review.
Exact Influence-band thresholds and the conversion ledger weights (how much Influence a shinden plot vs a paid debt vs a won petition is worth) — defer to the balance facet/§4.
Does Influence ever decay or only stall? (Recommend: never decays passively; a small set of scripted dents only, all recoverable.) Confirm against the 'no wipe' feel.

Single big-number representation: does the koku/Influence economy reach magnitudes needing scientific/abbreviated number formatting across five tiers? (Carried over from the mechanics brainstorm's open question.)

Pacing lock: should the authored mystery (6 acts) and the tier climb be tuned to climax together at Tier 5/Edo, or does the mystery resolve at Tier 4 (Domain) with Tier 5 as a quieter 'restoration victory lap'? Affects narrative facet.

Needs a new ADR to supersede the D-004 'one diegetic reset' framing with 'tier escalation replaces prestige; no reset' — this is a lock-it-with-the-human item per CLAUDE.md, since it changes what the game is at the macro level.

facets.facet: Facet B — The Three-Faction Rank/Reputation System (Estate rank ladder, Village reputation, Origin family/friends standing) and their interplay
facets.summary:
Three parallel standing tracks, each earned diegetically by labour and quests, but mechanically distinct so they never feel like one bar painted three colours:

1. ESTATE RANK (主家 — the MAIN spine, a discrete RANK LADDER, not a float). Estate standing is the ONLY one of the three that is a named, gated *rank* with hard thresholds and big content gates, because rising through it IS the perseverance fantasy and it paces the tier climb. Eight ranks across the local-estate tier: Stray (taken-in, no wage) -> Day-labourer (hiyatoi) -> Bonded hand (genin) -> Trusted hand -> Foreman of works (kogashira) -> Bailiff of the home fields (jitō-dai) -> Steward's deputy (yōnin-hojo) -> Chief steward / lord's right hand (karō-kaku, the top a non-born man can plausibly reach by merit). A capstone path (merit-elevation toward retainer status, or symbolic adoption into the house name) is the bridge that unlocks the NEXT tier (estate -> village -> region -> domain -> Edo). Each rank costs Estate Service (a faction-XP pool from completed duties + survived seasons) AND requires named "trust beats" (authored story gates: e.g. defend the grain store, return the lost ledger, broker a deal). Each rank UNLOCKS areas (which buildings/grounds you may enter), responsibilities (new auto-producer/management systems), systems (which panels reveal — Estate rank IS a primary driver of the UI-as-progression ladder), and story. This is the load-bearing track.

2. VILLAGE REPUTATION (里 — a continuous, MULTI-NODE reputation web, not a single ladder). The village is deliberately structured DIFFERENTLY from the estate: instead of one rank, it is several smaller standing meters — per-shop "regular" standing, per-family goodwill, an Artisans'/craft-guild standing, and the Village Chief's overall regard which is a weighted roll-up of the others. You raise these by buying/selling, completing open-ended help (a family's flooded paddy, a craftsman's missing tool, a festival), and by the Inn's RUMOURS board (light folklore side quests). Village standing unlocks RECIPES, TRADE (better prices, exclusive goods, the market-saturation damper softens), CRAFTING TIERS, ALLIES (idle helpers / co-producers), and LORE — never UI/tier gates (so a player who ignores the village is never *blocked* on the spine, only poorer and lonelier). The kamikakushi legend lives here: the village's regard for "Tama" gives a passive baseline goodwill the estate does not extend.

3. ORIGIN STANDING (縁 — a memory-gated, late-blooming SUPPORT track). Locked until memory returns (Act 2-3 threshold via the dream cadence). It is NOT grindable from day one; it is *re-engaged*, in authored beats, as you recover your past and travel down-valley/to the home town. It has a single "Restored ties" meter per recovered contact (family-by-marriage, old porter-guild mates, childhood friends), gated on memory milestones + travel + reputation elsewhere. Its payoff is explicitly SUPPORT, not local power: pride/morale (a global, modest skill-XP or producer buff justified as "working for something again"), ALLIES who plug into the estate's OUTWARD expansion, and TRADE TIES (origin-town goods/contracts that feed the estate's region/Edo ambitions). Crucially, per the locked guardrail, NONE of this is ever a *retroactive* mechanical gift from "remembering who you were" — every buff is framed as new relationships earned now, and returning memory itself grants ZERO stat/recipe/combat bonus.

INTERPLAY is the design's soul: the three are coupled by a shared economy and by the Tama-vs-farmhand allegiance, with deliberate tension AND synergy, and no faction is ever a dead end.

key_designs.name: Estate Rank Ladder — 8 named ranks + a tier-bridging capstone (the MAIN progression)
key_designs.description: Eight discrete ranks for the local-estate tier: Stray -> Day-labourer (hiyatoi) -> Bonded hand (genin) -> Trusted hand -> Foreman of works -> Bailiff of the home fields -> Steward's deputy -> Chief steward (karō-kaku). Promotion needs BOTH (a) an Estate Service threshold and (b) one or more authored trust-beats (story gates), so you can't grind a number to skip the narrative — rank is where 'numbers-go-up' and 'story' fuse. Each rank UNLOCKS a concrete bundle: AREAS (Stray=storehouse only; Foreman=workshops & granary; Steward's deputy=the lord's study & ledgers; Chief steward=the estate gate to the wider world), RESPONSIBILITIES (each rank hands a new managed sub-economy: a labour-gang to assign, then the granary to balance, then the estate's whole books — management depth scales with rank), SYSTEMS (Estate rank is the dominant UI-reveal trigger: the Skills tab, the assignment/management panel, the Influence macro-resource, and the tier-expansion map each reveal at a rank), and STORY (each rank earns the trust to hear the next confession in the side-mysteries). The capstone (merit-elevation / symbolic adoption) is the diegetic bridge to the NEXT tier — finishing the ladder doesn't reset anything; it OPENS the village tier as the new canvas (you now manage the estate AND project influence outward as the lord's right hand). This is the load-bearing track.

key_designs.name: Village Reputation — a multi-node web (shops, families, artisans, chief), not a ladder
key_designs.description: Village standing is intentionally shaped UNLIKE the estate so the two feel different in the hand. It is several continuous meters: per-shop 'patron/regular' standing (each shop unlocks stock & discounts as you trade — ties into the market-saturation damper from the mechanics doc: high standing softens the price-crash from flooding a good), per-family goodwill (raised by open-ended help, not checklists — you hear a family has trouble and go figure out how to help), an Artisans'/craft-guild standing (gates recipes, component tiers, master-craft commissions), and the Village Chief's regard (a weighted roll-up of the others + chief-specific quests). Village reputation unlocks RECIPES/CRAFTING TIERS, TRADE (prices, exclusives, contracts), ALLIES (villagers who become idle producers/helpers you can second to the estate), and LORE (rumours-board folklore + clues for the kamikakushi side-thread). It NEVER gates the UI ladder or the tier climb — the village is a rich optional faction that makes the spine easier and warmer, so skipping it is a viable-but-poorer playstyle, never a wall.

key_designs.name: Origin Standing — memory-gated, support-only, fuels OUTWARD expansion
key_designs.description: Locked behind the recurring-dream memory cadence (opens Act 2-3). Not a from-zero grind: it's re-engaged in authored beats as memory returns and as you can physically reach the home town (which itself depends on estate rank / influence to travel freely). Per-contact 'restored ties' meters (old porter-guild, surviving kin/friends). Payoff is deliberately SUPPORT not local rank: a modest global morale buff (skill-XP/producer multiplier, framed as 'a man with people behind him works harder'), ALLIES and TRADE TIES that specifically plug into the estate's region/Edo expansion (origin-town goods, contracts, a trusted partner for a distant trade route). HARD GUARDRAIL honoured: returning memory grants ZERO retroactive mechanical bonus; every Origin buff is a NEW relationship earned now, in the present, by effort — consistent with D-003 and the porter's-knot rule.

key_designs.name: Three distinct currencies + one shared macro-resource (Influence)
key_designs.description: Estate Service XP (duties + survived seasons), Village Reputation (per-node, from trade & help), and Origin Ties (memory-milestone + travel + present-day effort) are SEPARATE pools so the tracks don't collapse into one bar. Above all three sits INFLUENCE — the macro-resource from the locked vision — which the estate spends to expand outward. Estate rank generates Influence directly; Village allies and Origin trade-ties act as MULTIPLIERS/feeders on Influence rather than substitutes. This is what makes the side factions matter to the spine without being on the critical path: they don't unlock the next tier, they make conquering it faster and cheaper. Faction-XP curves reuse the mechanics doc's conventions: Estate Service uses a STEEP geometric curve with per-duty XP caps (forces breadth of duties + repetition, paces big rank gates), Village nodes use GENTLER linear/soft-cap curves (frequent small dopamine, encourages dabbling across many nodes), Origin uses DISCRETE milestone steps (each restored tie is a chunky one-time unlock, not a grind).

key_designs.name: Tama-vs-Farmhand allegiance — a leaning, not a lock; no faction becomes a dead end
key_designs.description: The allegiance choice (embrace the villagers' 'Tama' legend vs. accept the estate's 'just a farmhand' view) is expressed through dialogue and which faction you lean into. Leaning Tama gives a passive VILLAGE goodwill baseline (they see you as 'theirs') and unlocks Tama-flavoured village dialogue/quests, at a small friction with the estate (the estate finds the legend a superstitious distraction — slightly slower trust beats, never blocked). Leaning farmhand gives smoother ESTATE trust and pragmatic respect, at the cost of some village warmth and Tama-only lore. CRITICAL design rule: the choice rebalances *rates and flavour*, never *availability* — both factions remain fully completable on either lean (you just pay a bit more XP on the disfavoured side), so neither path is a dead end and the player can swing the lean over time. The Origin track is allegiance-neutral (it's about who he REALLY is, which is neither Tama nor merely-a-farmhand), and ironically resolving Origin softens both other leans by giving him a true self to stand on.

key_designs.name: Tension & synergy map across all three (the estate leans on the villages; the village claims him; the origin allies the estate)
key_designs.description: TENSIONS (chosen, not bugs): (a) Estate taxes/levies the village — high estate rank can hand duties that EXTRACT from the village (collect the rice quota), raising Estate Service but denting Village reputation, forcing a real choice (by the book vs. quietly soften it, which the side-mystery rewards). (b) The kamikakushi legend: the village wants 'Tama' to be theirs; the estate wants a reliable hand — being beloved in the village can make the estate wary of split loyalty, and vice versa. (c) Origin pulls him away (the home town) while the estate spine pulls him deeper in — re-engaging family costs time/travel the estate would rather spend on duties. SYNERGIES: Village allies become idle producers you can second to estate works; Origin trade-ties become the supply chains that make region/Edo expansion affordable; high Estate rank grants the standing/safe-passage to even reach the Origin town and to formally trade with it. The endgame thesis: the spine (estate) is restored AND surpassed FASTEST and most richly when all three are woven together — but it's still completable spine-only, just slower and lonelier.

key_designs.name: Mapping to incremental mechanics (XP curves, rank perks, gated content, UI reveals)
key_designs.description: Reuses the existing pure-core conventions from the mechanics doc. Each faction track is DATA (a faction registry: id, current XP/standing, rank thresholds, the rewards/unlock bundle per rank, display-conditions). Reputation already exists as a gating concept (S9/M9 'Reputation per faction'); this facet specifies the three concrete tracks, their curves, and their bundles. Estate-rank promotions fire through the universal rewards/unlock bus (the same bus driving narrative flags AND UI reveals), so a promotion simultaneously: pushes a diegetic log line ('The steward sets his seal to your name'), reveals the next panel/area, grants the perk, and advances story flags. Rank PERKS layer onto the stat model as base/additive/multiplier (Foreman: +1 assignable helper slot; Steward's deputy: +X% Influence/season; capstone: unlocks the tier-expansion map). All thresholds/curves live in content registries so the balance tables (time-to-rank, XP-per-duty) are GENERATED into docs/ per single-source-of-truth, and are headlessly regression-testable via the DEV play API (assert each rank fires at the intended GameState).

facets.how_it_serves_the_vision: The locked vision names the estate-rank climb and influence-rise as 'the perseverance fantasy at grand scale' and the pacer of the UI-as-progression reveal — this facet makes that literal. The Estate Rank Ladder is the spine: eight earned ranks from taken-in stray to the lord's right hand, each a sweat-bought promotion that opens a new canvas, with the capstone diegetically bridging into the next influence tier (estate -> village -> region -> domain -> Edo) so the tier escalation REPLACES prestige with no reset of any kind. The Village web and Origin track deliver the locked 'rich side faction' and 'third faction unlocked as memory returns' exactly as specified — meaningful but NOT the spine — by making them feeders/multipliers on the macro-resource Influence rather than gates on the critical path, so they enrich the climb without ever blocking it. The Tama-vs-farmhand allegiance is implemented as the locked 'roleplay, not character-creator' leaning that affects standing & flavour but makes no faction a dead end. Every track honours the mediocre-start / effort-only contract (standing earned by labour & duties; returning memory grants zero mechanical bonus), and folklore stays light (it rides into the Village track as the optional rumours board), per the locked design philosophy.
facets.ties_to_mechanics: Builds directly on the already-synthesised mechanics: 'Reputation + economy damper' (S9/M9) is the seam this facet plugs into — it specified per-faction reputation gating dialogue/content and a market-saturation damper, which this facet realises as three concrete tracks (the damper is softened by Village shop standing). Promotions and reputation gains flow through the universal rewards/process_rewards unlock bus and display_conditions already defined for dialogue/quests, so a rank-up is one event that drives narrative flags, UI reveals, and perks together (Pillar 3 'story and systems are one dependency graph'). All three tracks are pure-core DATA in core/content registries (faction defs, rank thresholds, reward bundles) consumed by core/unlock predicates and core/rewards — no DOM. Faction XP curves reuse the documented curve conventions (steep geometric + per-event caps for Estate Service to pace big gates; gentle linear/soft-cap for Village nodes; discrete milestones for Origin). Rank perks use the base/additive/multiplier stat layering and recompute-on-load rule. Save persists only non-derivable state (per-faction XP/standing, rank, unlock/story flags). Balance tables (time-to-rank, XP-per-duty, reputation-per-help) are GENERATED into docs/ and the DEV play API force-state helpers let pacing/unlock tests assert each rank/standing gate fires at the intended GameState (Pillar 5).
risks: Three tracks risk feeling like one bar in three colours. Mitigation: deliberately differing SHAPES (estate=discrete gated ladder, village=continuous multi-node web, origin=discrete memory-milestone steps) and differing payoff TYPES (estate=UI/area/tier gates, village=recipes/trade/allies, origin=global support buffs feeding outward expansion) — but this must be enforced in content, or balance drift could homogenise them.

Estate being the ONLY UI/tier gate could make the village/origin feel skippable to the point of pointless. Mitigation: tune Influence so the side feeders give a large enough multiplier that ignoring them roughly doubles time-to-next-tier — felt, but never a hard wall. The exact multiplier needs a pacing playtest.

The estate-leans-on-village tension (tax/levy duties denting village rep) could punish the player for advancing the spine, breeding resentment rather than meaningful choice. Mitigation: always offer a 'soften it quietly' option (rewarded by the side-mystery), and cap how much spine progress requires anti-village acts.

Origin track's support buffs risk reading as a retroactive 'remembering made me stronger' gift, violating D-003. Mitigation: author every Origin buff as a NEW present-day relationship/contract, with explicit text, and keep memory-return itself perk-free. Needs a writing+systems guardrail review.

Eight estate ranks across only the local tier could pace too slowly or balloon the wait between gates (mechanics doc warns: never let the next goal exceed ~2-3x the prior). Mitigation: interleave rank gates with smaller responsibility/perk drips so the climb has texture between the big promotions.

Memory-gated Origin unlock could leave the third faction's whole UI dark for hours, feeling like a missing third of the game. Mitigation: foreshadow it diegetically (the dream, log lines) before it unlocks so its absence reads as anticipation, not omission.
open_questions: Exact number of estate ranks for the LOCAL tier — eight proposed; does the human want fewer/larger gates or more/smaller steps? And do later tiers (village/region/domain/Edo) each get their own fresh rank ladder, or does the protagonist's personal rank cap at the estate while higher tiers track INFLUENCE rather than personal rank?

How big should the side-faction multiplier on Influence be — i.e. how much faster is the tier climb with the village+origin woven in vs. spine-only? (Needs the pacing playtest the mechanics doc already flags.)

Should the Tama-vs-farmhand lean be a continuous slider that drifts with each choice, or a small set of discrete leanings (Tama / neutral / farmhand)? And can it be freely re-swung, or does it harden after key story beats?
Does the estate ACTIVELY penalise high village standing (jealousy/split-loyalty) as a live mechanic, or is that tension purely narrative flavour? The former adds depth but more balance surface.
Where exactly on the act/memory timeline does the Origin track unlock, and is there a minimum estate rank required to physically reach the home town (coupling Origin to spine progress)?
Do village ALLIES seconded to the estate count against village production (a real loan with a cost) or are they free helpers? The former makes the synergy a genuine trade-off; the latter is simpler.

ADR housekeeping: this facet assumes D-004 (one reset) is REVERSED by the locked 'no reset / tiers replace prestige' direction — confirm the reversal is recorded as a new ADR so the faction tier-bridge design isn't built against a stale decision.

facets.facet: Facet C — Cast across the three factions + the declining-estate backstory
facets.summary: A full dramatis personae for Kamikakushi, grouped by faction and area, each figure carrying a clear gameplay FUNCTION (quest-giver / rank-gatekeeper / mentor / rival / relationship / antagonist) and most doing double duty. The ESTATE (the main faction, the spine) gets a fuller 13-name household forming a legible rank-ladder the player climbs from taken-in stray to the top. The VILLAGE is a rich side faction of shopkeepers, craftsmen, legend-keepers and an innkeeper-with-a-rumours-board (the light-folklore delivery vector). The ORIGIN is the protagonist's LIVING family and friends in the nearby post-town of Magome-juku, re-met as memory returns and who become allies/trade ties that plug into the estate's outward expansion. Plus regional officials/rivals met as influence climbs the tiers. The declining-estate backstory is locked to a concrete, grounded cause two generations back (a flood-control venture that collapsed into ruinous debt + loss of domain favour after a cover-up) which the main quest overcomes. NOTE: this reconciles with existing canon (PRD §1, story-spine) but the LOCKED DIRECTION supersedes it on three points — (1) NO reset/prestige, replaced by the estate->village->region->domain->Edo tier climb; (2) the origin faction is the protagonist's LIVING family/friends in a town (not a drowned hamlet / dead family); (3) the estate is a POPULATED household, not the old "single aging retainer." Several beloved existing names are retained and re-slotted.

key_designs.name: ESTATE FACTION (MAIN QUEST) — the Kurosawa goshi household: summary + rank-ladder
key_designs.description: SUMMARY: The Kurosawa are a lower-samurai (goshi) house holding a modest hill estate in Asagiri valley — proud, threadbare, two generations past their grandeur and quietly drowning in debt. This is the MAIN faction and the spine: the player rises through its service hierarchy from taken-in stray to its effective right hand, and the estate's reputation track (call it 'Standing') gates the tier climb (estate->village->region->domain->Edo). Make it feel POPULATED. The diegetic RANK-LADDER the player climbs (each rung gatekept by a specific named NPC, which is how UI/systems unlock): (0) Stray / 'another mouth' -> (1) Field hand / day-labourer -> (2) Trusted hand & porter -> (3) Houseman / errand-and-escort -> (4) Junior retainer (ashigaru-tier, allowed a spear) -> (5) Retainer of the household -> (6) Trusted man / the lord's right hand / acting steward-in-training. The household embodies the effort-over-talent thesis collectively: a small, tired house that survives on grind, not glory.

key_designs.name: Lord Kurosawa Munenori — head of house (rank-gatekeeper, final main-quest authority, relationship)
key_designs.description: Late 50s, the current goshi lord. Weary, decent, stiff-backed pride papering over shame at the house's decline (his FATHER'S venture caused it — see backstory). Believes in duty and ledgers, not omens; to him the protagonist is 'a farmhand we took in,' full stop (the estate's grounded stance on the kamikakushi legend). FUNCTION: the apex rank-gatekeeper — promotions to the upper rungs (4->5->6) require his explicit recognition, earned by sustained service and by quietly helping the house out of debt. He is the man whose approval IS the main quest's measure. Arc: from defending a dying dignity to daring, late, to rebuild and expand outward (the tier climb is literally him being persuaded/enabled to reach again). Slow-warming relationship; his eventual trust is the emotional payoff of the climb.

key_designs.name: Kurosawa Naoyuki — the heir/son (rival -> ally, peer rank-gatekeeper, relationship)
key_designs.description: ~22, the lord's son and heir. Talented, restless, chafing at the genteel poverty he was born into; resents that a found stray is given a place and watches him with prickly suspicion. FUNCTION: the EARLY-GAME RIVAL inside the household — he gatekeeps the mid rungs (he must vouch for you before his father will), and at first he withholds. He's the talent-foil INSIDE the family: things came easily to him, so he disdains plodding effort — until the protagonist's relentless grind starts visibly outpacing his coasting. Arc: rivalry -> grudging respect -> genuine brotherhood and shared ambition; by the upper tiers he and the protagonist drive the expansion together (he wants Edo standing for the family name; you want it for the people you've come to care about). One of two embodiments of an ANTI-thesis that gets converted: talent humbled into earned partnership.

key_designs.name: Lady Kurosawa Chiyo & the dowager Kurosawa Toku — the lady of the house + the retired elder (relationship; lore/backstory keeper; soft gatekeeper)
key_designs.description: TWO household women doing distinct work. LADY CHIYO (~50, the lord's wife): manages the inner household and its meagre purse, gracious but exacting; FUNCTION: gatekeeps 'houseman' access (rung 3) and the household's domestic economy (textiles, kitchen, guest-facing duties) — winning her regard unlocks indoor work and the estate's hospitality/influence-entertaining systems used at higher tiers. DOWAGER TOKU (~75, the lord's mother, the retired matriarch): sharp-memoried, the only one who lived through the house's fall as an adult. FUNCTION: the living BACKSTORY KEEPER — she holds, and slowly parts with, the truth of why the house declined (the grandfather's flood-venture and the favour lost). A relationship/lore quest-giver, not a gatekeeper; she also gently embodies 'no shortcuts' (she rebuilt the household's daily survival by hand after the fall) and is wry about the village's 'kami-returned-Tama' fuss.

key_designs.name: Chief Steward Genemon (karo-like) — the household's right hand (PRIMARY rank-gatekeeper, recurring assigner of labour, the man whose job you ultimately grow into)
key_designs.description: ~60, the karo-equivalent chief steward who actually RUNS the estate day to day — its books, its quotas, its discipline. Dry, overworked, fiercely loyal to the house, exhausted by holding a sinking ledger together. FUNCTION: the SPINE'S main rank-gatekeeper and quest-giver — he is the one who first calls the protagonist 'another mouth, soft and clumsy,' assigns nearly all early labour, and grants the rung-by-rung promotions (1->2->3->4) as you prove out. The single most-interacted estate NPC. Arc: from grudging tolerance to relying on you, to (at the top of the climb) the protagonist becoming his trusted deputy and eventual successor as the house's right hand — the literal 'rise to the top.' He is also a clean knot in the mystery: the estate's old rice-debt records pass through him (see village/origin links), so trust with him later opens the documentary thread.

key_designs.name: The retainers — Saburozaemon (stern senior), Heisuke (friendly peer), Kanbei (jealous rival), Tokujiro (green recruit)
key_designs.description: FOUR named samurai retainers of varying rank/age/temperament, the social cohort the player joins and must rise through. SABUROZAEMON (~55, stern senior retainer): traditional, exacting, withholds approval — gatekeeps respect at the retainer rungs; warms last and means it most. HEISUKE (~30, friendly peer): the open-handed one who shows you the ropes of retainer life and becomes a true friend — low-friction quest-giver and morale anchor; the 'we're in this together' counterweight. KANBEI (~35, the jealous rival): a middling retainer who sees the rising stray as a threat to his own scarce advancement — the in-household ANTAGONIST-RIVAL who sabotages, sneers, and spreads doubt; not evil, just cornered and proud (a frightened middle-man echo). Arc: either grudging detente or he washes out by his own bitterness — player-influenceable. TOKUJIRO (~16, green recruit): joins AFTER you've risen, looks up to you; FUNCTION: the mirror that proves your growth and seeds the late-tier 'you now teach others' layer (replacing the old reset's teaching idea) — he's who you start from zero, the way Jubei once started you.

key_designs.name: The household staff — Jubei (drillmaster/mentor), Tanomo (accountant/clerk), Oai (head maidservant), Kyusuke (cook), Sota & Mago (grooms/field labourers), Ranpo (physician)
key_designs.description: The non-samurai staff who give the house texture and run its working systems. JUBEI (retained from canon, re-slotted as the estate MASTER-AT-ARMS / drillmaster): aging, competent-but-never-great old foot-soldier with bad knees; THE MENTOR and combat/training gatekeeper who speaks the creed 'Talent is a story the lucky tell. You are not lucky. So you will work.' — the chief embodiment of effort-over-talent; gates the entire training/idle-combat suite after the humbling first fight. TANOMO (~45, the accountant/clerk): keeps the estate's books; FUNCTION: gatekeeps the koku/economy and debt-repayment systems and (crucially) is the in-house thread into the ledger/debt mystery — helping him balance the books is how the player materially saves the estate and uncovers old entries. OAI (~40, head maidservant): runs the indoor staff and the valley's servant-gossip network — quest-giver and information broker INSIDE the house. KYUSUKE (~50, cook): warm comic-relief and the food/provisioning sub-economy; a soft daily-life anchor. SOTA & MAGO (a grizzled groom and a cheeky teen field-labourer): the bottom-rung peers the protagonist starts beside — the field/stable labour loop and honest friendship at the floor of the ladder. RANPO (~55, the estate PHYSICIAN): dresses the protagonist's healing scalp wound (grounding the mundane amnesia), names symptoms not visions, gates healing/medicine systems; the rational voice that flatly disbelieves the kami story.

key_designs.name: VILLAGE FACTION (rich side faction) — Asagiri village: summary + reputation track
key_designs.description: SUMMARY: Asagiri village sits below the estate — farmers, craftsmen, shops, an inn, a shrine. It has its OWN problems the player may choose to help (a feared ford, a skimming foreman, debt to the estate, the unhealed grief over the lost child), a Village Chief reputation track, and it is the home of the KAMIKAKUSHI / 'are you Tama?' legend — the villagers BELIEVE the protagonist is their child Tama, returned by the kami, while the estate flatly does not. The player's allegiance choice ('Tama' vs 'a farmhand') is expressed largely through how he leans into THIS faction vs the estate. Folklore lives here as LIGHT flavour: the inn's rumours board is the delivery vector for optional, lighthearted yokai side-tidbits the player can go investigate (each resolving grounded). The village faction's deepest side-quest is uncovering what really happened to the real lost child.

key_designs.name: Village leaders & legend-keepers — Chief Yagoemon, Sayo, Obaa Sato, Priest Ryoa
key_designs.description: CHIEF YAGOEMON (the village headman/shoya, retained & re-slotted): the Village Chief reputation gatekeeper — grants village standing and access to village-tier expansion. He is ALSO the humanly-reachable culprit later (a frightened middle-man who doctored ledgers and let the 'cursed' story stand) — double duty: warm civic quest-giver up front, reachable answer-for-it figure at the climax. SAYO (retained — the heart of the legend): ~16, sharp and kind, the chief's daughter (re-slotted from 'steward's daughter' since the estate now has its own steward family) and keeper of the valley's news/rumours; FUNCTION: the village's main quest-giver, the emotional anchor, and the LIVING HEART OF THE RED HERRING — she names him 'Tama' on sight and needs him to be her lost friend. The person the truth most hurts and most frees. OBAA SATO (retained — herbalist/wise-woman): the village's folklore-keeper who narrates the kamikakushi legend sympathetically while knowing it's coping; teaches foraging/village-craft; the warm face of the village's belief. PRIEST RYOA (the shrine keeper): runs the soul-calling rite and keeps a register of the vanished — folklore-atmosphere quest-giver whose records become hard evidence; embodies grief-work that yields truth.

key_designs.name: Village shops, craftsmen & artisans — Smith Gonta, Carpenter Risuke, Weaver Onatsu, Brewer Tokuemon, Innkeeper Sukezo, Peddler Sokichi, Foreman Magobei
key_designs.description: MULTIPLE named shopkeepers/artisans, each a sub-economy quest-giver and a crafting/trade gatekeeper that the estate's outward expansion later plugs into. SMITH GONTA (gruff blacksmith): tools & later spearheads — gates metalcraft; values shown effort over flash. CARPENTER RISUKE (carpenter/builder): repairs and construction — gates building systems used to rebuild/expand the estate. WEAVER ONATSU (weaver, a sharp widow): cloth/textile trade — ties into the lady's household economy and origin-town trade routes. BREWER TOKUEMON (sake brewer, jovial): the village's festival/social hub and a flavour-rich relationship; his brew greases reputation. INNKEEPER SUKEZO: runs the inn and the RUMOURS BOARD — the dedicated delivery vector for optional light-folklore side-quests (each rumour a lightweight yokai story to go investigate, resolving grounded); information broker. PEDDLER SOKICHI (retained — itinerant medicine-seller): the engine that lets the player chase clues and trade BEYOND the village (the bridge toward the origin town and the regional tier); grounded source of yokai tales. FOREMAN MAGOBEI (the skimming foreman, the 'tanuki'): notable ordinary-villager-turned-petty-antagonist — a side-quest culprit whose theft is the first hard thread into the larger racket; reachable and human.

key_designs.name: ORIGIN FACTION (side faction, unlocked as memory returns) — Tahei's family & friends in Magome-juku post-town: summary + how it plugs in
key_designs.description: SUMMARY (RECONCILED to LOCKED DIRECTION — supersedes the old 'orphaned, family dead, drowned hamlet' canon): the protagonist's true name is Tahei; his ORIGINAL life was in MAGOME-JUKU, a modest post-town a day-plus down the road (NOT the local village), where he worked as a young porter/errand-hand for a small transport-and-goods house. He LEFT on a mundane errand — escorting a goods consignment over the mountain pass for his employer — and the journey went wrong (a flash flood / rockfall on the fog-blind pass struck the small caravan; head trauma + near-drowning + exposure -> ordinary amnesia). His family and friends are ALIVE and, once he re-engages, SUPPORT him with pride, allies, resources, and trade ties that feed the estate's outward expansion. FUNCTION: a side-quest line unlocked by the recurring DREAM thread (memory-only, never clairvoyant, zero mechanical bonus) and by reaching the regional tier; reconnecting opens TRADE ROUTES and ALLIES that are concretely useful to the spine (the origin town becomes a node in the estate's region->domain->Edo expansion).

key_designs.name: Origin family & friends — Tahei's mother Oyuki, sister Okiku, employer Master Denbei, friend Kanta, sweetheart Ohana, the guildmaster Zenroku
key_designs.description: OYUKI (~45, Tahei's widowed mother): the emotional core of the origin thread — she grieved him as dead; the reunion is the warm payoff of the memory arc (kept earned and a little costly — she's furious he was written off, and at first he barely remembers her). OKIKU (~20, his elder sister): practical, married into a Magome trading family — FUNCTION: the concrete TRADE-TIE that lets the origin town supply/route goods for the estate's expansion. MASTER DENBEI (~55, his old employer, runs the small goods house): proud of the diligent boy he trained; supplies the porter/logistics know-how and the legitimate manifests — quest-giver and the grounded source of the 'porter's-knot' identity (he taught it; it confers ZERO bonus). KANTA (~18, childhood best friend, fellow porter): comic-warm friendship rekindled; an ally who can be recruited to help the expansion. OHANA (~17, a sweetheart he half-remembers): optional relationship thread that the dream surfaces; grounded and gentle, no melodrama. GUILDMASTER ZENROKU (~50, the transport guild boss who WROTE TAHEI OFF): the origin faction's ANTAGONIST — he abandoned a missing porter to avoid admitting his caravan was running short-weighted/contraband cargo; reachable later as a witness/culprit, tying the origin thread into the same rice-quota racket as the spine. Embodies the world's casual disposal of the small.

key_designs.name: Regional officials & rivals (met as influence climbs the tiers) — Tedai Kuroiwa, the enforcer Hanzaki, Clerk Naozane, the rival house Tomita, the touring Inspector
key_designs.description: The antagonist-and-rival cast that scales with the tier climb (village->region->domain->Edo), reused from canon and extended. TEDAI KUROIWA (retained — the deputy magistrate's agent): the PRIMARY human antagonist of the regional tiers — polite, patient, dangerous; architect of the tax-quota cover-up and racket; benefits from the comforting myth; opposes the estate's rise because a rising house asks questions. Defeated by evidence, not violence. HANZAKI (retained — the scarred ronin enforcer, the 'one-eyed mountain god'): recurring physical threat and the talent-gone-rotten MIRROR (his edge is trained + brutal, NEVER innate — the thesis holds); survived by trained endurance, never out-talented. CLERK NAOZANE (retained — guilt-sick junior clerk): the crack in the cover-up; supplies documentary proof; a reachable conscience. RIVAL HOUSE TOMITA (new — a competing goshi/merchant house that prospered as the Kurosawa fell, partly by exploiting their debt): the inter-house RIVAL on the regional/domain tier — a status-and-influence competitor the player must out-maneuver, not kill; embodies 'talent + luck + ruthlessness' against the Kurosawa's earned comeback. THE TOURING INSPECTOR (a senior domain/Edo official): the apex authority reached only at the top tiers — the figure to whom the osso-style petition is made and before whom real recognition/standing at the capital is finally won; the gate on the Edo climax.

key_designs.name: DECLINING-ESTATE BACKSTORY (locked, grounded) — 'the Two Generations' fall + the thread that overcomes it
key_designs.description: CONCRETE GROUNDED CAUSE (2 generations back): the protagonist's arrival-era lord is Munenori; his FATHER kept the house barely afloat; the fall traces to the GRANDFATHER, Kurosawa Sadamune, two generations prior. Sadamune, ambitious to restore the house's standing, championed and personally funded a major FLOOD-CONTROL / land-reclamation VENTURE — a new embankment-and-paddy scheme up the valley meant to win domain favour and create taxable land. He mortgaged the house against it (ruinous debt). The venture was botched/under-maintained; when a monsoon came the works failed, a poor upstream hamlet was lost, and to save face the disaster was blamed on the kami ('an impious, unlucky place') while ledgers were doctored to hide that relief funds had been diverted. The house lost DOMAIN FAVOUR after the cover-up was half-suspected, was saddled with the debt that funded the failed venture, and shrank from a respected estate into the threadbare goshi house of today. THE EMOTIONAL STAKES & THESIS: the same debt-and-quota machine that ruined the Kurosawa is the root that menaces Asagiri village and that wrote off Tahei's caravan — so saving the SPECIFIC people the player comes to care about (Sayo's grieving village, the loyal tired Genemon and Jubei, the proud broken lord, Tahei's own family) is the same act as restoring the house. WHO EMBODIES 'EFFORT OVER TALENT / NO SHORTCUTS': Jubei (the creed, primary), the dowager Toku (rebuilt daily survival by hand), Master Denbei (made a diligent boy of an orphan), and the protagonist himself; the ANTI-thesis (talent/luck/shortcuts) is embodied and either converted (heir Naoyuki, rival Kanbei) or defeated (Hanzaki, House Tomita, Guildmaster Zenroku). The MAIN QUEST overcomes the fall by grinding the house out of debt, restoring its name through evidence/justice and labour, and climbing the tiers to surpass even Sadamune's lost grandeur — ending in real recognition at Edo.

facets.how_it_serves_the_vision: The cast is built so the ESTATE is unmistakably the spine and the PERSEVERANCE-AT-GRAND-SCALE fantasy. Every estate NPC is a rung or a gate on an explicit service ladder (stray -> field hand -> trusted hand -> houseman -> junior retainer -> retainer -> the lord's right hand), so 'rise through the ranks to the top' is concrete and legible, and each promotion is a diegetic unlock of UI/systems — exactly the incremental 'the interface is the reward' pillar. The household is now genuinely POPULATED (13 named figures across lord/heir/lady/elder/steward/four retainers/master-at-arms/clerk/maid/cook/grooms/physician), fixing the old 'single aging retainer' thinness while keeping every loved canon character (Jubei, Sayo, Obaa Sato, Sokichi, Kuroiwa, Hanzaki, Naozane, Yagoemon) reused and re-slotted. The TIER CLIMB (estate->village->region->domain->Edo) replaces the now-cut reset: the village shopkeepers, the origin town's trade ties, the rival House Tomita, and the touring Inspector are deliberately positioned as the larger canvases that open as influence expands, climaxing in Edo recognition. The three factions stay cleanly differentiated: the estate DISBELIEVES the kamikakushi legend (he's just a farmhand), the village BELIEVES it (he's Tama) — making the player's 'Tama vs farmhand' allegiance a real, faction-weighted roleplay choice — and the origin faction (living family in a town, per the locked redesign) gives the memory thread a warm, supportive, mechanically-useful payoff that feeds the spine's expansion rather than a bleak dead-end. The grounded declining-estate backstory ties all three factions and the antagonists to ONE rotten root (the debt-and-quota machine), so helping specific people the player cares about IS restoring the house, giving the rise real emotional stakes.
facets.ties_to_mechanics: Every named figure maps to a system. RANK-GATEKEEPERS (Genemon, Lord Munenori, heir Naoyuki, Lady Chiyo, Saburozaemon) drive the estate Standing track that diegetically unlocks panels/tabs — the UI-as-progression bus. ECONOMY/CRAFT gatekeepers (clerk Tanomo for koku & debt-repayment; cook Kyusuke for provisioning; smith Gonta, carpenter Risuke, weaver Onatsu, brewer Tokuemon for village crafting; Okiku/Denbei for origin-town trade routes) hang the numbers-go-up production loops off specific relationships, and the estate's debt is the macro 'influence' sink the grind pays down. COMBAT/CONDITIONING is gated entirely behind Jubei (training suite unlocks only after the humbling first fight), with Hanzaki as the recurring auto-resolving threat whose outcomes are explicitly a function of labour-built conditioning — honoring 'combat is the rare exception' and 'no hidden edge.' FOLKLORE-AS-LIGHT-FLAVOUR is delivered through Innkeeper Sukezo's RUMOURS BOARD as optional, non-checklist yokai side-tidbits (Foreman Magobei/'tanuki', the feared ford, etc.), each resolving to a grounded cause via investigation in the world — satisfying the open-ended, non-hand-holdy quest philosophy. The MYSTERY/EVIDENCE layer is distributed across reachable culprits (Yagoemon, Magobei, Guildmaster Zenroku, eventually Kuroiwa) and conscience-witnesses (Naozane, Priest Ryoa's register, dowager Toku's testimony, clerk Tanomo's ledgers), all feeding the osso-style petition to the touring Inspector at the Edo tier. The DREAM thread (origin faction unlock) is memory-only with ZERO mechanical bonus, and the porter's-knot (taught by Denbei) is an identity clue conferring nothing — both guardrails preserved. The teaching/management layer that the cut reset used to provide is re-homed onto green recruit Tokujiro and recruited origin friends (Kanta) at the upper tiers: 'a man who climbed once now starting others from zero.'
risks: Cast bloat: ~35 named figures is a lot for a v1; many (especially mid-tier retainers, several village artisans, some origin friends) should be deferrable/optional content so the early game stays minimal per the 'start lean' preference. Recommend tagging each as core vs later-tier in §5.

Direct conflict with locked ADRs that must be re-decided with the human: this design SUPERSEDES D-004 (one reset) with the tier climb, and overturns the story-spine's 'origin = dead family / drowned hamlet Kuzuhara' with 'origin = living family in Magome-juku post-town.' These need new ADRs (the LOCKED DIRECTION authorizes them, but the decision log must be updated, old entries marked REVERSED).

Re-slotting Sayo from 'steward's daughter' to 'village chief's daughter' (because the estate now has its own steward family, Genemon) is clean but must be propagated consistently through the spine doc and PRD §1, which still say 'steward's daughter.'

The protagonist's true name is locked as Tahei (ADR D-006) but I introduced origin-family names (mother Oyuki, sister Okiku) and a friend 'Kanta' — note 'Okiku' collides phonetically with the village survivor 'Kiku' from old canon; since the drowned-hamlet/Kiku thread is largely superseded by the living-origin redesign, recommend either dropping canon-Kiku or renaming the sister to avoid confusion.

Edo-period authenticity load: goshi household titles (karo is really too grand for a goshi — 'chief steward / yonin-class' framing is safer), naming conventions, and the rank-ladder terms need a research pass before authoring so the household reads correct without lecturing.

Antagonist nuance: with several reachable culprits (Yagoemon, Magobei, Zenroku, Kanbei, Tomita, Kuroiwa, Hanzaki) there's a risk of a 'rogues gallery' that flattens into mustache-twirlers; each needs the canon's 'frightened/cornered human' treatment to keep the partial-justice theme honest.

Two embodiments of converted-talent (heir Naoyuki, rival Kanbei) risk redundancy; consider differentiating sharply (Naoyuki = family insider you win over; Kanbei = peer who may never come around) or merging.
open_questions: Reset vs tier climb: confirm with the human that D-004 (one grounded reset) is formally REVERSED in favour of the locked no-reset tier escalation, and capture as a new ADR (the LOCKED DIRECTION says so, but the decision log is authoritative and still says 'one reset').

Origin redesign: confirm the origin faction is LIVING family/friends in a post-town (Magome-juku, name TBD) and that the old 'orphaned porter, family dead, drowned Kuzuhara hamlet, fellow-survivor Kiku' material is superseded — and whether ANY of it (e.g. Kiku) should be salvaged into the village or origin faction.

Estate name and place names: is 'Kurosawa' (the estate, from canon) the locked house name? Confirm the village ('Asagiri') and origin post-town names; pick period-plausible names for the new figures (lord Munenori, heir Naoyuki, steward Genemon, etc.).

The real lost child ('Tama'): the locked direction keeps 'uncover what really happened to the lost child' as a strong village side-segment. Confirm the grounded resolution — is canon's 'Tama was a girl who ran and is alive (Oharu)' retained, and does it now connect to the estate's old rice-debt (the backstory's cover-up) as before?
Scope/altitude: how many of these ~35 names should exist in the v1 estate-tier slice vs be authored later? (Aligns with the user's 'start lean, add back deliberately' preference.)

Title-rank vocabulary: are we comfortable using lightly-stylized titles (chief steward, master-at-arms, junior retainer) rather than strict historical terms, and does the goshi household warrant a karo-equivalent at all, or should the top role be a humbler yonin/steward?

Allegiance mechanics: how strongly should the 'Tama' (lean village) vs 'farmhand' (lean estate) choice diverge — pure flavour/standing weighting, or does it gate any content? Facet C assumes flavour + faction-standing weighting only.

facets.facet: Facet D — Origin & Identity side-threads (the village's "Tama" legend; the protagonist's true origin & family; the recurring Dream) — woven through the estate-rise spine without overtaking it.
facets.summary:
Two grounded, optional-but-rewarding threads run quietly beneath the estate-rise spine and only ever pay off in feeling, allies, and flavour — never in mechanical power.

(1) THE VILLAGE'S LEGEND ("Are you Tama?"). The valley grieves a child, Tama, "spirited away by the kami" ~10 years ago. A half-drowned, memoryless young man washing up below the estate reads to them as the kami giving Tama back. The estate flatly does not believe it — to the lord and steward he is a stray farmhand taken in out of decency. This split is the engine of an ALLEGIANCE CHOICE the player expresses continuously through dialogue and which faction he leans into: lean "I am Tama" (embrace the villagers' hope) or "I'm just a farmhand" (the estate's view) — or stay non-committal. It is roleplay, not a flag-once branch: it nudges village vs. estate standing and changes flavour/dialogue, never stats. A strong, open-ended side segment lets the player uncover what REALLY happened to the lost child: Tama was a GIRL (the legend has drifted to "remembering" a boy — a fair, re-readable clue) who RAN from a violent stepfather and a household about to sell her into service to clear a rice debt; helped by a midwife and a peddler, she has been alive and grown in a down-valley post-town the whole decade. The adults let "the kami took her" stand because a comforting miracle was easier than "we were about to sell a child." No magic; every omen resolves to a human cause.

(2) THE TRUE ORIGIN (faction 3 — his real family & friends in a nearby town). His real past is mundane and sad: an orphaned tenant-farmhand-turned-PORTER from an upstream hamlet, indentured to a transport guild to work off a dead parent's debt, caught in a flash flood (neglected embankment) that smashed his caravan — blunt head trauma + near-drowning + exposure = ordinary amnesia. As memory returns in sensory fragments he can re-engage with the people from his old life: kin and old friends/guild-mates who, seeing what he is rebuilding at the estate, come to SUPPORT him with pride, allies, and trade ties that plug into the estate's outward expansion. (Note: the canonical spine kills his immediate family in the flood; per the locked 'they SUPPORT him' direction I recommend BROADENING faction 3 to surviving extended kin, a foster-sister, and old guild-friends so there are living people to reconnect with and to back the estate — see open questions.)

THE DREAM is the recurring thread that surfaces faction 3. It is strictly RETURNING AUTOBIOGRAPHICAL MEMORY — only things he actually lived (a face, a road, the smell of paddy-mud, a name in the water) — NEVER clairvoyance, never external facts he couldn't have known, never a mechanical bonus. It is reconstructed sensory memory (the voice in the water is a real person he heard, recalled — not a psychic tether), and it drips on a guaranteed cadence so the thread never goes cold.

Both threads ENRICH the spine: the legend gives the village faction its emotional weight and the allegiance lever; the origin gives faction 3 its reason to exist and a pipeline of allies/trade into the estate's tier-climb. Neither grants power; neither competes with the estate as the spine.

key_designs.name: The allegiance lever — 'Tama' vs 'a farmhand' as continuous roleplay, not a one-time branch
key_designs.description: A lightweight LEANING value (village-leaning ↔ estate-leaning, default neutral) that the player nudges through ordinary dialogue choices and which faction he invests labour in — NOT a character-creator and NOT a single locked fork. Leaning toward 'I am Tama' warms the village faction (the chief, Sayo, grieving households open up faster, give village-flavoured greetings, fold him into Bon rites) and very slightly cools the estate's view of him as a sober hired hand; leaning 'just a farmhand' does the reverse. Crucially it only ever shifts STANDING-GAIN FLAVOUR and which dialogue/quest-givers open, NEVER stats, drops, or production. The player can stay ambivalent the whole game (a valid, in-character stance: he genuinely doesn't know). The choice is re-expressible, not frozen — he can drift as he learns more. This is the mechanical home of the 'who you are is a SIDE thread, roleplay not creator' lock.

key_designs.name: Differential payoff per faction (so the choice MEANS something without power)
key_designs.description: Each lean pays off DIFFERENTLY and emotionally, never with raw bonuses. Leaning-Tama: the village embraces him as their returned child — warmer, faster village-rep gains, access to grief-coded content (the soul-calling rite, the mountain shrine, households who confide), and a heavier reckoning later when he uncovers Tama was a girl who ran (the village's hope curdles; Sayo, who most needs him to be her lost friend, is most hurt). Leaning-farmhand: the estate respects his honesty and self-made standing — the steward and lord trust an unpretentious hand quicker, estate-rank flavour reads as 'earned, not gifted', and the village stays at a fonder distance. Either way the SAME estate-rise spine and the SAME tier-climb are fully reachable; neither lean is the 'good' path. The payoff is in tone, which doors open first, and how the truth lands — not in numbers.

key_designs.name: The lost-child investigation as an OPEN-ENDED 'find things out', not a waypoint checklist
key_designs.description: Per the design philosophy, 'What happened to Tama?' is a SUGGESTION + A STORY the player optionally pulls on, then goes and discovers in the world — never a tracked A→B→C list. It surfaces from ambient texture (a child's straw sandal left yearly at the shrine; a household that flinches at her name; the legend 'remembering' a boy when an old woman misremembers 'she'). Clues are STUMBLED UPON through ordinary labour and travel and through rising village trust (which itself is earned by the grind): a midwife's reluctance, a peddler's road-gossip, a debt-ledger mention, the gender-drift, a post-town sighting. The player assembles the human truth (she ran; she's alive and grown) by noticing and asking, at his own pace. There is no quest arrow; there is a mystery you can choose to be curious about. It resolves grounded and partial — reuniting the valley with the living, grown Tama is costly and incomplete (she may not forgive the home that nearly sold her).

key_designs.name: Faction 3 (the origin) as an unlock-on-memory ally pipeline that feeds the estate's outward tiers
key_designs.description: His original family & friends from a nearby TOWN (explicitly NOT the local village) become a reachable SIDE faction as the Dream returns memory. Mechanically they are a third reputation/relationship track that OPENS only as origin-memory beats fire, and whose payoff is ALLIES, PRIDE, and TRADE TIES — resources/contacts that plug into the estate's expansion outward (village→region→town→Edo). Example: a recovered guild-friend or a surviving foster-kin in the post-town becomes a trade contact who unlocks a route or a buyer when the estate hits the regional tier — making the emotional reconnection diegetically FUEL the spine instead of competing with it. This honours the locked 'they SUPPORT him for his effort & achievements' beat: the people he rebuilds ties with show up proud and become standing/allies/logistics, never a flat power buff.

key_designs.name: The Dream system — guaranteed-cadence autobiographical drip, memory-only, zero bonus
key_designs.description: A recurring half-drowning DREAM is the thread that leads back to faction 3. Rules, enforced in writing AND code: (a) MEMORY-ONLY — it returns only things he actually lived (his name, the road, paddy-mud smell, the porter's-knot habit's origin, a name surfacing: the upstream hamlet, a person's voice). It NEVER surfaces external facts he couldn't have known firsthand (no clairvoyance, no precognition, no psychic tether). The 'voice in the water' is a real person he heard and is now recalling, not a spirit. (b) ZERO MECHANICAL BONUS — a returning memory or instinctive habit never grants a starting stat, recipe, tool, or combat edge (binds the porter's-knot = identity-clue-only rule). (c) GUARANTEED CADENCE — at least one origin/dream fragment is delivered within a capped number of in-game days OR a skill/standing threshold, and at least one origin-thread beat is always available WITHOUT reputation-gating, so the thread never fully stalls behind the grind. Each fragment is a short authored sensory beat in the event log; the renderer just shows the deterministic-core flag firing.

key_designs.name: Folklore handling for both threads — believe → human/natural cause; capped off-screen ambiguity
key_designs.description: Every omen/belief inside these threads is GENUINE, respected village dread first, investigated through ordinary work, and resolved one-to-one to a concrete human cause (the master 'kamikakushi' = a flood victim + a child who ran + negligence dressed as fate; offerings at the boundary-stone where he was found = an unidentified neighbour's grief). Debunk with DAWNING DREAD, never a Scooby-Doo unmasking; linger in the unease before resolving. Residual ambiguity is HARD-CAPPED at the project budget (≤1–2 unresolved, always off-screen, mundane-readable beats — recommend the unidentified-hand offering as the one left open), and is NEVER confirmed magic and NEVER mechanized. The Dream is explicitly de-supernaturalised (reconstructed memory, not a tether). Keep folklore LIGHT here: the legend is heavy because it's about real grief, but the broader yokai material stays as occasional light side tidbits (e.g. an inn rumours-board), not the spine.

key_designs.name: Tone & pacing guardrails so the side-threads never outweigh the estate spine
key_designs.description: The cozy estate/village labour loop stays the dominant minute-to-minute texture; the identity/origin material lands as occasional WEIGHT, not the mood. Darkest beats (a child nearly sold, a drowned hamlet, his family's fate) are kept OFF-SCREEN and reported via testimony/ledgers/a survivor's account, not depicted. Drip cadence is steady but sparse so curiosity never goes cold and never floods the screen. Both threads are framed as OPTIONAL enrichment — a player who only wants to climb the estate ranks can largely ignore them and still finish the spine, while a curious player gets a bittersweet, human story. The estate-rise remains the perseverance fantasy and the tier-climb remains the only escalation; these threads decorate it, they do not replace it.

facets.how_it_serves_the_vision: The locked vision is the estate-rise-as-perseverance-fantasy, grounded and magic-free, with 'who you truly are' explicitly a SIDE thread expressed as roleplay. Facet D delivers exactly that: it gives the two non-spine factions (the village, faction 3) their emotional reason to exist and their hooks into the player, while structurally subordinating both to the estate climb.
