The allegiance lever satisfies the 'choose to identify as Tama vs a farmhand, as narrative/allegiance choice, NOT a character-creator' lock precisely — continuous in-character roleplay that moves standing-flavour and which doors open, never stats. The Dream and origin thread honour the no-magic and mediocre-start locks absolutely (memory-only, zero bonus, ordinary trauma). And by routing faction 3's payoff into ALLIES + TRADE TIES that feed the outward tier expansion, the origin thread literally serves the spine's escalation (village→region→town→Edo) rather than competing with it — the emotional reconnection becomes fuel for restoring-and-surpassing the estate's grandeur. The anti-checklist, open-ended treatment matches the locked quest philosophy: a suggestion and a story to be curious about, discovered by living in the world, not a waypoint list.
facets.ties_to_mechanics: Built entirely on the existing pure-core systems with no new architecture: (1) The allegiance lever is one small core value updated by the universal rewards/unlock bus from dialogue choices and labour-faction investment; it feeds the existing per-faction reputation gating (estate/village/faction-3) only to change dialogue display_conditions and standing-gain flavour — never derived stats, keeping it lint-clean of any 'memory grants bonus' violation. (2) The lost-child investigation rides the existing dialogue+quest 'story/unlock bus' and reputation gates, but authored as ambient, conditionally-revealed textlines (clues surface on flags/standing/location, not as a linear task chain) — using the same data-driven display_conditions the catalog already specifies. (3) Faction 3 is a third reputation track + a set of unlock predicates whose rewards are location/route/contact/dialogue unlocks (allies & trade ties) that the tier-escalation consumes; it reuses the rewards object (locations/dialogues/flags/quests) with NO item/xp/stat rewards attached to memory beats. (4) The Dream is a scheduler-driven story-fragment emitter: the per-day scheduler (plans) checks a 'days-since-last-fragment' counter against a cap and fires an authored event-log line + a story flag, deterministically (no Math.random; flavour variance via the single seeded RNG). The guaranteed-cadence + always-one-ungated-thread rule is a tunable content constraint, headlessly regression-testable via the DEV __qa play API (force-state to a late standing, fast-forward days, assert a fragment fired within N days and that no stat/recipe was granted). All belief→cause pairings live in the existing folklore table; the ambiguity cap is enforced as a content-verifier check. Single-source-of-truth: the allegiance/flavour table and the clue/fragment registry generate into docs/ rather than being hand-maintained.
risks: NAME/RESET CANON DRIFT: The canonical spine doc and ADR D-006 lock the true name as 'Tahei' revealed in 'Act 4' and a season-cycle 'reset' (D-004). The new LOCKED DIRECTION replaces prestige/reset with a pure TIER climb and treats identity as a side thread. Facet D assumes the tier framing and a late-but-de-emphasised name reveal; the act-structure / reset language in the spine and D-004/D-006 needs a reconciling ADR or these docs will contradict the new direction.

FAMILY-DEATH vs 'THEY SUPPORT HIM' CONTRADICTION: The spine kills the protagonist's immediate family in the flood ('there was never anything heroic to go back to'), but the locked direction requires faction 3 to be living people who SUPPORT his estate achievements. Without broadening faction 3 to surviving extended kin / foster-sister / old guild-friends, there is no one left to reconnect with — a direct contradiction that must be resolved (recommend broadening, kept bittersweet).

ALLEGIANCE LEVER DRIFTING INTO POWER: Any future temptation to attach even small stat/price/drop bonuses to a lean would violate the 'roleplay not power, no memory bonus' locks. Must stay flavour/door-opening only; enforce with a code/content guardrail and a __qa test asserting no derived stat depends on the lean.

SIDE-THREAD OVER-SPINE: A rich mystery + a third faction + a recurring dream could pull the player's attention off the estate climb. Mitigate by keeping the threads optional, the drip sparse, and the estate spine the dominant loop; the estate ending must be reachable while largely ignoring these threads.

DREAM-AS-INFO-CHANNEL CREEP: Easy to slip an external fact into a dream for convenience, breaking the memory-only/no-clairvoyance lock. Needs an authoring checklist + the residual-ambiguity cap (≤1–2 off-screen) held firm; the one 'too-neat' dream-edge beat from the ambiguity budget must stay off-screen and mundane-readable.

SCOOBY-DOO / TONAL WHIPLASH on the lost-child reveal: 'Tama was a girl who ran' detonating the village's miracle can land as a gotcha or as tonal whiplash against the cozy loop. Plant the gender-drift and other fair clues early, debunk with dawning dread, keep the darkest material (near-sale, drowning) off-screen and reported.
open_questions: Faction 3 composition: do we BROADEN the origin faction to surviving extended kin / a foster-sister / old guild-friends (so there are living people to reconnect with and to back the estate, per the locked 'they support him' beat), while keeping the immediate-family loss as the bittersweet core? Recommend yes — needs a human nod since it touches the canonical spine.

Does the 'Tahei' true-name reveal and its timing survive the tier-reframe, and is it still a late beat now that identity is explicitly a side thread (not the spine)? Or is the name reveal softened/optional? Needs reconciling with ADR D-006.

Tier mapping for faction-3 payoffs: which specific outward tiers (region / castle-town / Edo) do the origin-faction allies and trade ties plug into, so the emotional reconnection lands as concrete escalation fuel at the right moment?

Exact Dream cadence numbers: the cap on in-game days (or the skill/standing threshold) between guaranteed fragments, and which one origin-thread beat is the always-available, non-reputation-gated entry — to be tuned via __qa pacing tests once the tick/scheduler exist.

Which single residual-ambiguity token (recommend the unidentified-hand offering at the boundary-stone) is the one left unresolved for these threads, given the project-wide ≤1–2 off-screen cap shared with the broader folklore content?

Should the allegiance lever surface ANY visible UI (a subtle standing/flavour indicator) or stay fully implicit in dialogue and which doors open? Implicit is safer for 'roleplay not creator' but may be too invisible to read as a meaningful choice — needs a design/playtest call.

facets.facet: Facet E — Edo-period authenticity & plausibility pass
facets.summary:
The grounded spine (declining goshi estate, kamikakushi red herring, neglected-embankment cover-up, rigged masu, osso petition, flood-displaced porter origin) is historically plausible and already strongly researched in brainstorms/2026-06-25-grounded-story-spine.md (authenticity notes, lines 366-393) and docs/prd.md §1. This pass VERIFIES those claims and hardens the single biggest accuracy risk: the LOCKED ambition to "restore AND SURPASS the estate's former grandeur, climaxing with real recognition at Edo," plus the LOCKED tier ladder (estate -> village -> region -> domain/castle-town -> Edo) used as the escalation that REPLACES prestige.

THE LOAD-BEARING TENSION: Edo status mobility was real but TIGHTLY CAPPED and SLOW (generational, money- and merit-gated, often via adoption/marriage), and it ran THROUGH the han (domain), never around it. A lone rural goshi household climbing in one young man's lifetime to "standing at the Edo capital" is the one place the design must FUDGE GRACEFULLY. The fix is to redefine the tiers as RISING WEALTH + INFLUENCE + REPUTATION (which genuinely scaled fast and far for gono/goshi via shinden reclamation, commercial crops, and merchant capital), while keeping FORMAL rank ascent slow, partial, and authentically mediated — so "Edo" is reached as INDIRECT recognition (a domain product famous in Edo; the lord's standing lifted via sankin-kotai; a shogunal/han appointment as the rare ceiling), NOT the protagonist personally becoming a hatamoto or being received by the shogun. This also harmonizes perfectly with the locked "no hidden talent / earned-only / partial-justice" pillars: the ceiling itself is the honest expression of "effort meets a real wall the powerful set."

Concrete do/avoid guidance below for each required area: the rise levers & ceiling, what "recognition at Edo" can authentically mean, the estate<->village relationship (reconciling the game's "village chief" with the real shoya/nanushi), the han->daikan->shoya chain, kaido/sekisho travel and why a flood is the right outsider-arrival mechanism, and respectful folk-religion texture (kamikakushi, jizo, shrine rites).

key_designs.name: Goshi status: keep it modest, and make the climb wealth+influence, not rank
key_designs.description: VERIFIED: goshi were rural lower samurai, above peasants and below castle-town (jokashi/hanshi) samurai; many were 'mudaka' (no stipend) and worked their own fields, with prosperity varying wildly by domain (Satsuma, Tosa, Higo all differed). A declining goshi household working its own land and extending rice debt to tenants is plausible and apt — keep it a single aging-retainer household, modest, NOT lordly. DO: drive the early-to-mid tiers (estate->village->region) almost entirely through WEALTH and LOCAL INFLUENCE — these scaled fast and far for rural samurai/wealthy farmers. AVOID: depicting formal RANK promotion as fast or self-directed; rank moved slowly, via merit recognized BY the domain, money, or adoption/marriage — never by a young man simply deciding to rise.

key_designs.name: Realistic rise LEVERS (use these as the diegetic engines of each tier)
key_designs.description: VERIFIED period-accurate levers, in rough order of authenticity-strength: (1) SHINDEN reclamation — developing new paddy was the canonical legitimate route to raise a household's effective kokudaka and standing; merchants even financed it (e.g. the Konoike new-field projects). This is the IDEAL spine of the estate-restoration economy. (2) COMMERCIAL/cash crops & a domain product (cotton, tea, silk, indigo, paper, wax, sake) — domains actively encouraged 'meibutsu.' (3) GONO dynamics — accumulating land, rural credit, and proto-entrepreneurship as a wealthy-farmer adjacent to the goshi estate. (4) GOYOKIN / contributions — buying favor/status by lending or 'contributing' to a cash-strapped domain (this is how commoners literally bought into samurai families). (5) SERVICE & PROMOTION within han administration (village/district office, then domain office). (6) ADOPTION/MARRIAGE up the status ladder — the most common real mechanism for a family to gain rank. DO: map the tier ladder onto levers 1-3 for the bulk of play, gate the top tier(s) behind 4-6. AVOID: any lever that grants standing for combat heroics or 'talent' (violates the locked no-gift pillar AND is ahistorical for a peasant).

key_designs.name: The CEILING and what 'recognition at Edo' can authentically mean
key_designs.description: This is the must-fudge-gracefully point. AUTHENTIC ceiling for a goshi household within one or two generations: regional prominence (a leading gono/goshi family of the district), a respected position in domain administration, and a famous product/achievement — NOT personal entry into the shogun's direct vassalage. Hatamoto (direct shogunal vassals, ~5,000 households) was effectively a closed, hereditary class a rural peasant-turned-goshi could not realistically join. DO frame 'Edo' as INDIRECT, mediated recognition, any of: (a) the estate's domain PRODUCT becoming renowned in Edo markets (carried there via sankin-kotai supply chains); (b) the protagonist's labor materially lifting the LORD's standing, so the HOUSE (not the man) gains face during the lord's Edo attendance; (c) a rare han/shogunal APPOINTMENT or commendation earned by distinguished, documented MERIT (the historically real but exceptional top rung); (d) a marriage/adoption tie that plants the family in a higher status orbit. AVOID: the shogun personally receiving/knighting the protagonist; the protagonist 'becoming a hatamoto' by his own hand; any rags-to-daimyo arc. The honest ceiling is itself thematic gold: it mirrors the locked PARTIAL-JUSTICE ending (the reachable is reached; the truly powerful remain above you).

key_designs.name: Reconcile the game's 'VILLAGE CHIEF' with the real shoya/nanushi
key_designs.description: VERIFIED: the Edo village headman was the shoya (western Japan) or nanushi (eastern Japan), drawn from local elite families, assisted by kumigashira (group heads) and hyakushodai (peasants' representative). Under MURAUKE, the village collectively subcontracted its own tax/admin obligations — villages were largely SELF-GOVERNING below the headman. DO: name the locked 'village chief' as the shoya/nanushi and let the player build reputation with him AND the village's self-governing body; this makes the 'village faction with its own problems' authentic (the headman juggles nengu quotas, corvee, disputes, relief). DO: this is ALSO the antagonist seam — the canon's Headman Yagoemon doctoring ledgers fits the real shoya role exactly (he is the reachable culprit, a frightened middle-man, not a monster). AVOID: a generic 'chief/mayor'; AVOID portraying the village as passively ruled — Edo villages ran themselves and negotiated upward.

key_designs.name: Estate<->village economic relationship (nengu, corvee, debt)
key_designs.description: VERIFIED: villages paid nengu (land tax) in rice, commonly ~40-50% of yield, collected via the headman under murauke; corvee/kuniyaku labor obligations were real and were exactly the kind of resource that could be DIVERTED (the canon's neglected upstream embankment vs. protected downstream tax fields is historically resonant with real flood-control politics). A goshi estate extending rice DEBT to tenants and holding local power over peasants is attested. DO: make the estate's relationship to the village one of tax-intermediation, labor mobilization, and rural CREDIT (debt is the 'one rotten root' the canon already uses to link the drowned hamlet to the near-sale of Tama). AVOID: feudal-Europe framing (no fiefs/knights/serfs vocabulary); the unit is the village collective and the koku/rice economy, not coin-rent manor.

key_designs.name: The administrative chain: han -> daikan -> shoya (and the tedai)
key_designs.description: VERIFIED: daimyo ruled domains (han); below them daikan (intendants/magistrates) handled local/district administration, assisted by tedai (clerks/agents); the village shoya/nanushi was the bottom intermediary. The canon's antagonist stack maps cleanly: Kuroiwa = the daikan's man (tedai), Headman Yagoemon = shoya, with a guilt-sick junior clerk (Naozane) as the documentary crack. DO: keep this exact chain as the institutional ladder the player must petition UP. DO: make the player's rising 'influence' literally about being heard higher up this chain. AVOID: inventing offices; use daikan, tedai, shoya/nanushi, and (for towns) bugyo. The osso petition (next item) is precisely the act of going OVER this chain's head.

key_designs.name: OSSO petition — keep the danger honest (most authenticity-sensitive mechanic)
key_designs.description: VERIFIED and reinforced: direct over-the-head appeals (osso / daihyo-osso) were ILLEGAL and genuinely lethal — petitioners were frequently EXECUTED EVEN WHEN their grievance was upheld (the gimin / martyr-petitioner pattern: wrongs redressed, the man who carried the petition put to death, sometimes with his family). DO: portray the climactic petition as the canon already intends — a deadly gamble that wins PARTIAL redress at terrible cost, not a clean courtroom victory; the reachable culprits answer, the untouchable magistrate escapes. This is the single best authenticity-and-theme convergence in the whole design. AVOID: any 'present evidence -> villain arrested -> justice served' beat; that would be both ahistorical and would betray the locked bittersweet/partial-justice tone. Consider that the protagonist (or an ally) bearing real personal risk for the petition is the authentic, earned climax.

key_designs.name: Kaido / sekisho travel & WHY a flood is the right outsider-arrival
key_designs.description: VERIFIED: inter-domain travel was checkpoint-controlled (sekisho), requiring travel permits ('no guns in, no women out'); the Five Routes had post-towns with regulated porter/horse relays; rivers like the Oi were deliberately kept bridgeless as natural barriers. A commoner could NOT freely wander across domain borders. DO: keep the locked origin — an indentured porter on a tax-rice/trade caravan, displaced by a FLASH FLOOD in the mountains — because uncontrolled displacement (not free travel) is the authentic way an undocumented outsider ends up across a boundary and at the estate's weir. This also grounds his amnesia (head trauma + near-drowning + exposure) with zero magic. DO: use sekisho/permits as a felt constraint when the player later ranges down-valley to the post-town (chasing Oharu). AVOID: the protagonist casually travelling between domains early; AVOID a tidy 'he walked here' backstory — the checkpoint regime makes the flood mechanism necessary, not just convenient.

key_designs.name: Folk-religion texture: kamikakushi, jizo, shrine rites — specific and respectful
key_designs.description: VERIFIED: kamikakushi ('hidden by kami') was a real, widely-held folk explanation for sudden disappearances ESPECIALLY of children and women — emotionally a way to make an unbearable loss 'survivable' (the missing one is safe with the kami, may return), recorded by Yanagita (Tono Monogatari). This is EXACTLY the canon's framing (a comforting lie covering a human truth) and is respectful when handled as grief-coping, not superstition-to-mock. DO: use SPECIFIC, correct texture — roadside/boundary jizo (guardians of children and travelers, fitting both the lost child AND the protagonist found at a boundary-stone), Bon offerings, shimenawa, a soul-calling/mukae rite, a priest's register of vanished travelers, ema, ofuda. DO: distinguish Shinto kami-veneration (shrine, shimenawa, boundary/mountain kami) from Buddhist elements (jizo, Bon, temple register) rather than blurring into generic 'Shinto.' AVOID: generic 'Shinto magic,' invented yokai-lore as plot physics, and any rite that mechanically WORKS — the locked rule is folklore is believed-atmosphere, every belief resolves to a human/natural cause, capped residual ambiguity, never confirmed magic, never player magic.

facets.how_it_serves_the_vision: It protects the LOCKED grand-rise vision from its one fatal authenticity flaw. The vision wants a satisfying climb that 'restores AND surpasses' the house and reaches 'recognition at Edo,' using a tier ladder (estate->village->region->domain->Edo) as the escalation that replaces prestige. History says formal rank mobility was slow, capped, and domain-mediated — so a naive reading (peasant becomes hatamoto / meets the shogun) would read as fantasy and quietly betray the grounded, no-magic, no-gift, partial-justice pillars. This pass keeps the BIG, escalating fantasy intact by routing it through the levers that genuinely DID scale far and fast for rural samurai and wealthy farmers (shinden reclamation, commercial crops/meibutsu, gono wealth, goyokin contributions, service, adoption/marriage), while redefining 'Edo' as indirect, mediated recognition (a famous domain product reaching Edo; the lord's standing lifted via sankin-kotai; a rare merit appointment). Crucially, the realistic CEILING is not a constraint to apologize for — it IS the vision's own emotional thesis made structural: effort takes a nobody astonishingly far, and then meets a wall the truly powerful built, exactly like the petition's partial justice. The honest history makes the perseverance fantasy land harder, not softer.
facets.ties_to_mechanics: The tier ladder (the locked prestige-replacement) gets a concrete, period-accurate economic engine: each tier is a documented rise-lever, which keeps the incremental 'bigger numbers / fresh canvas' loop diegetic. Estate tier = labor + first shinden plots raising the household's effective kokudaka; village tier = building reputation with the shoya/nanushi and the self-governing village while taking on nengu/corvee intermediation and rural credit; region tier = a commercial crop / domain product (meibutsu) and gono-style land/credit accumulation; domain/castle-town tier = service in han administration + goyokin contributions + adoption/marriage ties; Edo tier = INDIRECT recognition (product famous in Edo, lord's standing, a rare appointment). The koku/rice base economy and 'influence' macro-resource map directly onto real nengu, kokudaka, and the climb up the han->daikan->shoya chain. The osso petition is the climactic investigation/evidence mechanic (rigged masu, doctored ledger, corvee tally-stick, witness testimony) cashed in as a deadly gamble for partial redress — tying the investigation layer, the partial-justice ending, and historical accuracy into one beat. The sekisho/permit regime is a real gating constraint for ranging to the post-town. Folk-religion texture (jizo, Bon, soul-calling rite, kamikakushi rumor) is content/atmosphere that the belief->cause table converts into grounded quests, with the rumours-board (rare yokai side stories) staying light per the locked quest philosophy. Idle auto-combat stays the rare exception (vermin/bandits/mountains) and never grants standing — preserving the no-gift, effort-only pillar.
risks: Tier-overreach: if the top tiers let the protagonist PERSONALLY ascend to hatamoto/shogunal audience, the whole grounded promise collapses into power-fantasy. Mitigate by making 'Edo' indirect/mediated and the formal-rank ceiling explicit in the narrative.

Pace mismatch: real status mobility was generational (adoption/marriage/merit over decades), but incremental pacing wants visible same-session climbing. Risk that compressing decades into a lifetime reads false. Mitigate by climbing WEALTH/INFLUENCE/REPUTATION fast (authentic) while keeping FORMAL rank slow, partial, and clearly mediated — and by using the locked Act-5 season-cycle time-skip to absorb the longest jumps.

Osso softening: the genre's reward instinct pushes toward a clean legal win; history (and the locked tone) demand lethal risk and partial redress. Risk of an unearned 'justice served' beat. Mitigate by binding the osso outcome to the partial-justice guardrail in code/writing.

Folklore flattening: blurring Shinto/Buddhist elements into generic 'Shinto magic,' or letting a rite mechanically 'work,' would be both inaccurate and a breach of the no-magic rule. Mitigate with specific, sourced ritual texture and the belief->cause table.

Goshi-as-lordly drift: writing the estate as a grand manor with serfs would be wrong (most goshi were modest, often mudaka, working their own fields). Mitigate by keeping the starting estate humble (single aging retainer, declined fortunes) so the rise has real distance to travel.

Anachronistic vocabulary/speech: feudal-Europe terms (fief/knight/serf/mayor) or flat modern register. Mitigate with status-marked address (goshi household vs. tenant vs. daikan's agent) and correct office names (daikan, tedai, shoya/nanushi, bugyo).

Real-disaster pinning: tying the cover-up to a named Tenmei/Tenpo famine or real place invites accuracy nitpicks and insensitivity. Mitigate by keeping it generic-rural, as the brainstorm already advises.
open_questions: Which decade/sub-period is the canonical setting? Status-mobility texture, commercial-crop options, and famine resonance differ between early (1600s, more residual goshi), mid (1700s, gono/merchant capital rising), and late (1800s, Tenpo-era distress) Edo. A locked window would sharpen levers and meibutsu choices.

Which domain ARCHETYPE is the estate in? Goshi treatment varied sharply (Satsuma's many goshi vs. Tosa's discriminated goshi vs. Higo's zaichu gokenin); the fictional 'Kurosawa' domain should pick a coherent model so the rise-levers and ceiling are internally consistent. Keep it fictional/generic-rural but pick a template.

Should the top-tier 'Edo recognition' be the lord's standing, a famous domain PRODUCT, or a rare merit appointment — or a player-chosen branch among these? A product-meibutsu route ties most cleanly to the shinden/commercial-crop economy; needs a §2/§5 decision.

How explicit should the CEILING be in-narrative — i.e., does the game state outright that he can never personally become hatamoto/meet the shogun, or let it emerge through the partial-justice ending? An ADR may be warranted given it touches the locked ambition.

Does adoption/marriage-up become an actual late-game mechanic (a real historical lever) or stay narrative flavor? It is the most authentic top-rung path but introduces relationship/family-line systems that may exceed v1 scope.

Whose neck is on the line in the osso (the protagonist, an ally, or a gimin-style martyr NPC)? The most authentic and affecting option is real personal risk; needs a §5 narrative decision consistent with the partial-justice guardrail.

facets.summary:
The world of Kamikakushi is Asagiri-dani, a single mountain valley, expanding by tiers outward to the realm. It is organized as 5 area-GROUPS that double as the spine's tier ladder (estate -> village -> region -> domain/castle-town -> Edo). Each group is a cluster of distinct, separately-unlocking AREAS (rooms/spaces) that anchor the three faction tracks, the cast, the labour economy, and the human mystery.

GROUP 1 — THE KUROSAWA ESTATE & GROUNDS (faction 1, the spine; Tier 0-1). The starting micro-world, unlocked room-by-room as trust rises. Areas: (a) the KURA STOREHOUSE where he wakes — the Act 0 cell, one verb ("rake the spilled rice"); (b) the GATE & FORECOURT (genkan) — the social threshold where rank is granted and visitors arrive; (c) the MAIN HOUSE / OMOYA — Jubei, Sayo's family, the steward, lord & heir; dialogue and the rank track live here; (d) the HOME PADDIES & DRY FIELDS — the rice/koku heartbeat (farming node); (e) the DRILL YARD — opened by the Act 1 humbling defeat; conditioning/idle-combat training under Jubei; (f) the STABLES & WOODLOT edge — minor labour (hauling, firewood) and the porter's-knot beat. Activities: rice farming, hauling, fieldwork, then drills. The estate-rank climb is the main quest measured here.

GROUP 2 — THE VILLAGE OF ASAGIRI (faction 2, rich side faction; Tier 1-2). Unlocked when the estate trusts him to run errands. A cluster of social/economic areas around the kamikakushi legend: (a) the MARKET / SHOP ROW — the smith (tools), the dry-goods/rice broker, the herbalist Obaa Sato's stall (trade + coin economy reveal); (b) ARTISAN WORKSHOPS — smithy, charcoal/carpentry, dyer/weaver (craft chains + apprenticeship side quests); (c) the CHIEF'S HOUSE (shoya Yagoemon) — the village reputation track AND the doctored-ledger thread; (d) the INN with its RUMOURS BOARD — the hub for light, optional folklore side quests (each rumour a yokai tidbit the player may go investigate open-endedly); (e) the SHRINE / TEMPLE — the soul-calling rite, the priest's register (a mystery clue), Bon offerings; (f) the boundary-stone / jizo where he was found (the unidentified-hand ambiguity beat). This is where the "are you Tama?" legend lives and is believed.

GROUP 3 — THE SATOYAMA & MOUNTAINS (shared wilderness; Tier 1-3). The exploration and danger gradient, gated by conditioning. Concentric rings: (a) the near SATOYAMA — managed forest/foraging (sansai, mushrooms, herbs, woodcutting, bamboo); (b) the RIVER, FORD & WEIR — fishing node + the "kappa" thread (undertow, smugglers' sinking-spot) + the weir where he was snagged; (c) the FOOTHILLS & charcoal grounds — the "fox-fire" kiln, Genza's range, hunting; (d) the FORBIDDEN UPSTREAM RUINS of KUZUHARA — the drowned hamlet (Act 3), Kiku, the broken seki, his own origin clues; (e) the HIGH MOUNTAINS & THE PASS — the lethal terrain where he was found, the "one-eyed mountain god" (Hanzaki's lookout), expeditions/logistics. Reward is open-ended discovery, not waypoints.

GROUP 4 — THE ORIGIN: KUZUHARA & THE POST-TOWN (faction 3, his old life; Tier 2-3). NOT the local village. Two linked places: (a) KUZUHARA itself (overlaps the upstream ruins) — his birthplace, recovered as memory returns; (b) the DOWN-VALLEY POST-TOWN (shukuba on the kaido) — where the grown, living "Tama" (Oharu), the peddler Sokichi, and his old porter-guild ties survive; the clerk Naozane's office sits near here. This faction SUPPORTS him for his estate achievements — pride, allies, trade ties that plug into estate expansion. Reached via the road; the recurring dream is the thread that leads here.

GROUP 5 — THE EXPANSION TIERS: REGION, CASTLE-TOWN, EDO (Tier 3-5). The fresh, larger canvases the influence climb opens, each a bigger-numbers escalation with NO reset. (a) the wider DISTRICT/REGION — multiple villages, trails, river network the estate's influence now spans (logistics/holdings layer); (b) the CASTLE-TOWN / DOMAIN SEAT — the daikan's office, the inspector, the formal petition (osso), inter-domain trade, where the estate's standing is contested at the domain level; (c) EDO — the capital, the climax: real recognition and standing for the restored-and-surpassed house. Each tier is a macro-area set, not a single room; the tier climb is the long-game escalation.

For each area I specify: what's there, which faction(s) and cast inhabit it, what activities/resources/quests/yokai-rumours it hosts, and roughly when it unlocks against the act/tier ladder — keeping discovery open-ended throughout.

key_designs.name: Five area-groups ARE the tier ladder (estate -> village -> region -> domain -> Edo)
key_designs.description: The world is structured so the spatial groups double as the locked tier-escalation. Group 1 (estate) is Tier 0-1, Group 2 (village) Tier 1-2, Group 3 (satoyama/mountains) is the cross-cutting wilderness spanning Tiers 1-3, Group 4 (origin) is Tiers 2-3, and Group 5 is the macro-tiers (region 3, castle-town/domain 4, Edo 5). Climbing influence literally unlocks a new, larger spatial canvas — there is NO prestige/reset; the map physically grows. This directly implements the locked 'tier-based escalation replaces prestige' decision.

key_designs.name: Estate as a room-by-room incremental micro-world (the diegetic-UI engine's home turf)
key_designs.description: Group 1's six areas (kura -> forecourt -> main house -> paddies -> drill yard -> stables/woodlot) unlock ONE AT A TIME as rank/trust rises, each reveal a story beat in the event log. Act 0 is literally one room and one verb (rake spilled rice in the kura). This makes the estate the embodiment of the 'UI itself unlocks' pillar at the spatial level: the player's accessible space IS the progress bar of the main-quest rank climb.

key_designs.name: The Village INN + RUMOURS BOARD as the folklore-as-light-flavour delivery system
key_designs.description: Per the locked design philosophy (folklore is light flavour, not the spine), folklore is concentrated in one diegetic hub: the village inn's rumours board. Each rumour is a lightweight, optional yokai tidbit (kappa at the ford, tanuki stealing rice, fox-fire on the ridge, yamanba of the high woods, tengu of the cedars) that the player MAY go investigate out in the world, open-endedly. This keeps named yokai off the critical path while giving the satoyama/mountains texture and reasons to explore — every rumour resolves to the locked belief->cause table (a human/natural cause).

key_designs.name: Concentric danger-ring wilderness gated by conditioning, not waypoints
key_designs.description: Group 3 (satoyama -> ford/weir -> foothills -> upstream ruins -> high pass) is a single continuous gradient of increasing danger and reward. Access to deeper rings is gated by labour-built conditioning (you can't survive the pass until months of hauling gave you a back and wind), honouring the mediocre-start contract. Areas reward stumbling-upon and discovery (a recovered weighted bundle at the ford, the broken seki upstream, Genza's camp) rather than tracked checklists — implementing 'open-ended, non-hand-holdy quests'.

key_designs.name: Three factions mapped to three distinct place-groups (estate / village / origin-town)
key_designs.description: Each faction track has a spatial home so allegiance is expressed by WHERE the player invests. Faction 1 (estate, the spine) = Group 1 + the rank climb. Faction 2 (village, side) = Group 2, its shops/chief/inn, and the 'Tama' legend the village believes. Faction 3 (origin) = Group 4, the down-valley post-town and Kuzuhara, reached as memory returns and supporting him for his estate achievements. The estate does NOT believe the legend; the village does — the spatial split makes the identity roleplay choice ('Tama' vs 'a farmhand') legible through which places he leans into.

key_designs.name: Kuzuhara as the geographic knot where all three threads physically meet
key_designs.description: The forbidden upstream ruins (in Group 3) ARE Kuzuhara (Group 4, his origin) AND the drowned hamlet whose neglected seki ties to the rice-quota cover-up (the spine's dark root). One location resolves the kappa-ford evidence, the protagonist's origin, Kiku, and the conspiracy. This co-location is deliberate: it lets a single late-tier area unlock (Act 3) detonate the village's legend, recover his past, and expose the machine the estate is complicit in — keeping the map small and the web legible per the cast's 'double-duty' design rule.

key_designs.name: The boundary-stone, ford-weir, and pass as the three mundane-mystery landmarks
key_designs.description: Three specific small sites carry the grounded mystery's atmosphere: the BOUNDARY-STONE/jizo where he was found (the capped unidentified-hand ambiguity beat), the FORD & WEIR (where he snagged + the 'kappa' smugglers' sinking-spot, physically recoverable evidence), and the high PASS (where the caravan died + the 'one-eyed god' lookout). These anchor the dream-thread and the investigation in real places the player visits, never in magic.

facets.how_it_serves_the_vision: The vision is a perseverance fantasy at grand scale: a nobody rises through an estate's ranks and expands its influence outward until the house is restored and surpassed at Edo, with the UI itself unlocking as the reveal. The world design makes that climb SPATIAL and therefore tangible. The player starts trapped in one storehouse room and earns the rest of the estate room-by-room (the rank climb made of doors); then earns the village (errands), then the wilderness (conditioning), then his own past (memory), then the region, the domain seat, and finally Edo. Because each tier opens a fresh, larger canvas with bigger numbers and NO reset, the map physically embodies the locked tier-escalation: influence is something you can SEE growing as the world you can reach grows. The three faction-place groups give the side threads (the village's kamikakushi legend, his origin) real geography without letting them become the spine — they are places you may visit, not waypoints you must clear. Folklore stays light because it is quarantined to the inn's rumours board and the wilderness it sends you into, while the estate-rise stays central because it owns the spine's tier-0-through-Edo backbone.
facets.ties_to_mechanics: Areas hang directly on the existing mechanics catalog and unlock ladder. (1) Each AREA is a location node in the core's location graph (core/content/locations) with an unlock PREDICATE over game state — exactly the UI-reveal engine; revealing an area pushes a diegetic log line, identical to panel reveals. This maps to ladder stages S3 (leave the storehouse), S6 (village), S9 (wider satoyama/map menu), S11 (mountains). (2) Each area HOSTS gathering nodes and producers: home paddies = rice/koku farming (S2 producer, the koku spine); satoyama = foraging/woodcutting (S3/S8); river/ford = fishing (S9); village = trade/coin + craft chains (S6/S8 charcoal->forge->tools). (3) The drill yard gates the auto-resolving combat suite (S7) and is itself unlocked by the Act 1 humbling defeat — combat areas (foothills, ruins, pass) feed the deterministic auto-battler with weighted population tables. (4) Faction reputation (S9, per-faction: estate/village/origin) gates which areas and which area-NPC dialogues open — trust-as-currency literally buys map access. (5) Expeditions/logistics (Act 3) and the tier macro-areas (region/domain/Edo) extend the same location-graph + producer model at a higher floor instead of the superseded M12 prestige reset — the tier climb replaces the reset entirely. (6) Everything stays pure-core: areas are data registries with predicates; the renderer only shows unlocked nodes; all randomness (which rumour, loot, encounter) flows through the single seeded RNG. (7) Area/unlock tables are generated into docs/ from the same registries (single source of truth).
risks: RESET vs TIER-CLIMB conflict in source docs: the brainstorm spine and PRD §1 still describe an 'Act 5 season-cycle RESET' (ADR D-004), but the LOCKED direction prompt explicitly REPLACES prestige/reset with the tier climb (estate->village->region->domain->Edo) and says 'NO RESET OF ANY KIND.' This facet follows the locked prompt (tier climb, no reset), which means ADR D-004 and PRD §1.7-1.8 (Act 5 reset) need a new superseding ADR. Flag for the human — this is a direction-changing decision the docs have not yet caught up to.

Scope/area-count explosion: ~25+ distinct areas across 5 groups is a lot to author, balance, and reveal-pace for a v1. The mechanics doc's own open question ('how many of ~12 areas ship in v1?') is unresolved. Risk that the outer tiers (region/domain/Edo) become thin or never ship. Mitigation: treat Groups 1-3 (estate/village/wilderness, ~Tiers 0-2) as the v1 spine and Groups 4-5 as later-tier content, but this needs an explicit v1-scope cut.

Naming/geography drift: the spine doc uses 'Asagiri valley' and 'Kurosawa estate' in some passages but the superseded synthesis used 'Akiyama estate'; the protagonist's true name flipped Ren->Tahei (D-006). Area/place names must be locked in one registry to avoid contradictions once content is authored.

Folklore-creep risk: even quarantined to the rumours board, a satoyama/mountains full of yokai-named sites could pull folklore back toward the spine, violating the 'light flavour' philosophy. Needs a cap on how many rumour-quests exist and a rule that none gate main-quest tier progression.

Tier escalation must keep bigger-numbers MEANINGFUL without a reset: replacing prestige with pure tier-jumps risks either runaway inflation or each tier feeling like a flat re-skin. The balance model (cost=base*1.15^owned, 5x tier jumps) was designed around a reset; it needs re-derivation for a no-reset tier ladder.

Identity-roleplay legibility: expressing 'Tama vs farmhand' purely through which faction-places the player leans into is elegant but subtle — risk the player never notices the choice. May need explicit dialogue surfacing at the village shrine / estate forecourt.
open_questions: Does ADR D-004 (one diegetic season-cycle RESET, Act 5) survive, given the locked direction's 'tier climb REPLACES prestige, NO RESET of any kind'? This facet assumes the tier climb fully replaces the reset — needs a superseding ADR to make the docs consistent.

What is the v1 area scope? Ship Groups 1-3 (estate + village + near wilderness, Tiers 0-2) complete and stub/defer Groups 4-5 (origin town, region, domain, Edo)? Or a thinner vertical slice through all five tiers?
How granular is the estate room-unlock (Group 1)? Six separate area-reveals, or do some (stables/woodlot) fold into the forecourt to avoid reveal fatigue in the early game?

Is the post-town (Group 4) reached on the SAME kaido road that nearly killed him (thematic) — and does crossing a sekisho checkpoint gate it (authentic, per the spine's note that flood displacement, not free travel, is how he arrived)?

Do the macro-tiers (region/domain/Edo) get full sub-area maps, or are they more abstract 'holdings/influence' boards layered over a thinner location set? This decides how much spatial authoring the late game needs.

Where exactly does the village/estate boundary sit on the believe-the-legend axis spatially — i.e., are there shared/neutral areas (the ford, the road) that belong to no faction and stay accessible regardless of allegiance?
How many inn rumour-quests (light yokai tidbits) ship, and is there a hard rule that ALL of them are optional and NONE gate tier progression (to protect the 'folklore is light' philosophy)?
Does the canonical estate name lock as KUROSAWA (spine doc) over the superseded Akiyama, and is the valley canonically ASAGIRI-dani? These need to be fixed before any location registry is authored.
